Before trying to pass through the Wind-stopping Swamp, I hope that the Master and Her Highness can show convincing skills. Otherwise, no one would be able to save them from the Wind-Stopping Swamp should unforeseen circumstances arise…”

“You want to test the Master’s skills?” Zhong Guan suddenly felt that this guy was a lunatic. If he wasn’t a lunatic, why would he leave the position of a City Lord improperly and become a guard? If he wasn’t a lunatic, how could he, as a guard, try to test his own Master’s strength? Is this something that he had the authority to do?

“You’re just courting death!” Hei Tu and Bai Ma were also very angry. As for the Hua Ban and the Flying Locust, they were ready to beat him up.

The identity of the Third Young Master shouldn’t be doubted by a mere guy like him.

He should have just stuck to being a proper pathfinder, but he chose to doubt the Third Young Master’s abilities? If the Third Young Master was not strong enough, would they leave everyone here to ensure their safety and advance to the Wind-stopping Swamp with Her Highness? He made this decision to show that he had enough confidence but Feng Ji doubted this instead— what a hopeless idiot!

Yue Yang waved his hand and asked Hua Ban and the others to retreat.

He made another gesture, “Come on, I’ll let you punch me and see if you can measure my strength!”

Feng Ji naturally knew that doing this would seem very disrespectful, but no matter how bad it was, it was much better than dying in Wind-Stopping Swamp! He gritted his teeth and gathered his strength. Of course, he did not dare to use all his strength, but secretly reserved 30% of it, so as not to hurt this ‘Master Titan’, who was known to be so honourable. After getting Yue Yang’s prompt again, he punched him.

Halfway.

Suddenly, he made an abrupt switch and aimed his attack at Princess Qian Qian.

Princess Qian Qian didn’t want to pay too much attention to it, but now she had noticed the persistence of this pathfinder thief. Sometimes, persistence was not a good thing, but if the starting point was good, then it would definitely not be a bad thing.

She stretched out a finger and lightly caught Feng Ji’s 70% punch.

At first, he thought that her strength was similar to his own, so he was stunned while Sha Tong and Ouba’s jaws dropped directly.

With just one finger, she could block Feng Ji’s sneak attack. How powerful was this Princess? But she didn’t seem to be a Heaven Ranker, so what’s going on?

Feng Ji first stayed still for three seconds before he reacted. He bent down and bowed to Princess Qian Qian, sincerely apologising, “Your Highness, Feng Ji just wanted to test your strength and reaction. In the Wind-Stopping Swamp, there are dangers at any time, so…” Before he could finish speaking, he jumped up and threw a punch at Yue Yang. This time, he did not make any reservations and used all of his strength to attack him.

In the Wind-Stopping Swamp, there were many powerful and unpredictable Heaven-Ranked beasts. If he couldn’t even take this sneak attack, then it’s better for him not to go to the Wind-Stopping Swamp!

Yue Yang also stretched out a finger, but his movement seemed slow.

Slow enough for anyone to see clearly.

Including Feng Ji, who made the sneak attack. The very slow and gradually moving finger, inconceivably bypassed Feng Ji’s lightning-fast fist. Instead of defending himself like Princess Qian Qian, he directly counterattacked.

With a flick of his finger, he flicked it on Feng Ji’s forehead.

In the next second, Feng Ji flew out like a cannonball, lashing out 10,000 metres away as he broke dozens of trees and crashed into the faraway mountain with a bang.

Ouba, Sha Tong and the others were so frightened that their knees went weak and their bodies trembled. They wanted to immediately kneel in front of the strong man to plead themselves guilty, but they were afraid that doing so would provoke the other party’s unhappiness.

Not knowing how long it took, Feng Ji crawled out from the depths of the buried mud pit like a dead dog.

He even spat out the remnants of dirt that was stuck in his mouth.

Feng Ji bowed and saluted in admiration. While suppressing his excitement, he shouted, “I was offensive, but this test gives me peace of mind. With your strong abilities and Her Highness’s, plus my role as a pathfinder, I can say that there is at least a 60% chance of passing through the Wind-Stopping Swamp. If I find that person again, with his dog nose and my cat’s eyes working together, the success rate can even be increased to 80%. Even if we fail, I think we can escape safely as your strengths are the most powerful guarantee! Master Titan, are you willing to hire another hunter from the Dog Clan? I dare say that it wouldn’t cost much as long as he can feast on meat and bones in every meal!”

Yue Yang nodded, “It doesn’t matter if you spend some money, but we must pass the Wind-stopping Swamp.”

Feng Ji was overjoyed when he heard it, and regardless of the dirt all over his body, he turned over and jumped on the back of the long-legged Dromaeosaur. Waving his hand, he said, “Master, Your Highness, let’s go!”

“Wait a minute!” Zhong Guan and the others suddenly surrounded them.

“What do you want to do? The Master himself agreed to my test just now and I didn’t hurt the Master either!” Feng Ji found that something was wrong. These guards who let him steal the limelight didn’t want to take revenge on him in public, right?

“What the Master said is beyond doubt, so of course we have no opinion about that; it’s just that you joined our team of guards. As usual, we have to welcome the newcomers!” Zhong Guan patted Feng Ji’s shoulder with a smile on his face with a ‘I am your boss and will cover for you’ enthusiasm. Feng Ji’s heart was hot, so he jumped down quickly and greeted them one by one, “Little brother has not greeted the bosses just now, but I’m too impatient— what is the welcome ceremony? When the task is over, let me invite the bosses for a drink! “

“Don’t be in a hurry to have a drink. Now is the welcome time for a newcomer and the ceremony is very important! Us guards have a fine tradition…”

Zhong Guan punched Feng Ji in the face, knocking him down. Hei Tu, Bai Ma and the others also gathered around to beat him furiously.

They only stopped when they got tired.

Feng Ji wanted to cry but there were no tears flowing out— what kind of newcomer welcome ceremony is this? To hell with that, this was simply abusing a newcomer!

However, Zhong Guan reached out to him and pulled Feng Ji up. He patted his clothes and said earnestly, “Because you have a mission right now, you can’t delay the Master’s time too much, so the next step of our welcome ceremony will be temporarily postponed. When you come back, we will definitely welcome you again. Don’t cry, in fact, your welcome ceremony has already been calculated. This makes me think back on the good old days…young man, the tradition must be inherited as it is a good tradition to carry forward!”

Feng Ji now understood what was going on. It turned out that these guys had been beaten as they were joining, so once a new person joined the crew, they would automatically be beaten.

Come on, such a tradition… Of course, this fight could only be a waste of time as he wiped a tear and nodded solemnly to Zhong Guan, “Captain, don’t worry; when a newcomer joins, I will definitely welcome him and let him understand what a fine tradition is!” At the end, Feng Ji gritted his teeth, wishing that a newcomer would join immediately, so that he could vent the anger in his heart.

“It’s good that you understand!” Zhong Guan smashed Feng Ji’s face with his big fist, and when he fell to the ground, he kicked wildly with his big feet, “Welcoming newcomers is the welfare of every senior team member. You have to remember this point well!”

“Don’t fight for too long, just for another ten minutes!” Yue Yang never objected to this tradition. In fact, he was the first founder of this tradition.

Sometimes, the more one hated something and the angrier they were, the more motivated they were willing to work.

So as the feeling increased, his expectations increased.

Hence, he would become more patient as well.

Although not all of the things in the world could be defined the same way, most of the things were…

chapters.title: Chapter 924
chapters.body:
Chapter 924

Chapter 924: Enemies? A Rather Puzzling Aura

The long-legged Dromaeosaur was galloping at high speed through the mountains, hills, and cliffs easily, as if they were walking on solid ground.

If one was capable of ignoring the fact that he had a fear of heights, Feng Ji was indeed a good pathfinder. He had extraordinary eyesight and could clearly see birds and beasts that were more than ten kilometres away. He could also clearly see the flapping wings of a fly when it flies. The most important point was that he had a strong instinct when it came to predicting danger. As soon as he found that something was wrong, he would quickly detour and correct his path forward.

In addition to his extraordinary talent in reconnaissance, he was also a good player in terms of stealth.

His mount, the inconspicuous long-legged Dromaeosaur, also had the special ability to instantly change its colour and camouflage, quickly integrating with the environment.

“Although this guy isn’t as good as Yan Zao, he’s not bad at all when it comes to pathfinding. HOwever, it really is such a shame for him to be afraid of heights— he’s a Quasi-Heaven Ranker!” Princess Qian Qian became slightly baffled after assessing Feng Ji.

She and Yue Yang held hands and flew in the sky at a slow pace. They were more than a thousand metres apart from their guide and followed from a distance.

Sometimes when she was happy, she would pull him over to sit on the rocks of the mountains that they flew by.

They would watch the clouds rise and fall in the sky, the slanting wind and light drizzles on the ground; it was a moment for the two of them to bask in.

If she got tired, she laid on Yue Yang’s back and let him teleport his way while she closed her eyes and rested temporarily.

“Maybe it’s because of the psychological trauma of his childhood…” Yue Yang analysed as he put on a farce, implementing the ‘I’m a psychology expert, but please don’t worship me too much’ persona.

“What about you? Is it also because of a psychological trauma of your childhood that made you so lustful?” Princess Qian Qian suddenly asked.

“Ah, that was gifted to me upon birth!” Yue Yang felt like he was about to sweat profusely.

“Do you think it’s prideful to shout about being born horny?” Princess Qian Qian really didn’t expect this kid to admit it.

“About this, being born lecherous is better than being born as a eunuch. At least, lechery could be reflected on and occasionally pretend to be a Liu Xiahui [1] to see if any beautiful ladies offer themselves onto my lap; but if I was a eunuch, that wouldn’t be possible. Haven’t you heard of it? Once you enter the palace gate, you get stuck in a place that is as deep as the sea— no matter how awesome a eunuch was, he would only become the Emperor’s right-hand man at most!” Yue Yang argued with a crooked reason.

T/L Note:[1] Liu Xiahui was a Chinese politician known to have the greatest morals and decency. Nowadays, his name can be used to describe someone as a gentleman.

“I think it was a mistake trying to talk to you,” Princess Qian Qian gave him a sullen look and waved her hand deliberately, trying to break free from Yue Yang’s grasp on her hand. However, student Yue Yang had seen many seniors and their experiences when picking up girls before he transmigrated into this world. One of the secrets was that when a man held the hand of a beauty, don’t let go when she wanted to break free. Women were peculiar creatures with duplicity, that is— what they say and what they truly wanted might be just the opposite. If a beautiful woman told them to let go and they just did so, or asked them to release her and followed without second thoughts, then that person wasn’t a man, but a mere worm who did everything he was told! Who would be willing to fall in love with a worm that only followed instructions? Who would spend a lifetime with a worm like that?

T/L Note: No one should purposely do something against another person’s will or consent. The top paragraph is said for comedic effect, but if someone is clearly in the wrong, please speak up about it regardless of the ‘gender’ privileges.

“Then let’s not discuss this and talk about other things. For example, a tigress said last night that she wanted to take a bath with me. Who would have thought that despite spending so long soaking in the tub to the point where the outer layer of my skin already peeled off, there was no one to be seen. Now, if I can trouble the honourable Tigress to give me a good explanation,” Yue Yang asked with a smile on his face, not seeming to be angry.

“Do you want to hear the truth or the lie?” Princess Qian Qian asked Yue Yang a multiple-choice question.

“The lie,” Yue Yang chose an option that ordinary people wouldn’t choose. Sometimes, by playing cards that ignored common sense, he would get very good results.

“Who in the world likes to listen to lies? Are you kidding me on purpose?” Princess Qian Qian couldn’t help but want to give him a punch, but after thinking about how comfortable it was to sleep on his back just now, she couldn’t just bring herself to hit the man who basically served her just because she replenished her energy and rest. Forget it, I’ll forgive him a little this time. Besides, this topic was originally provoked by me anyways.

“It doesn’t matter what I want to listen to, what matters is whether there will be some form of compensation tonight?” Yue Yang exclaimed that he was benevolent and forgiving, always thinking about the future rather than lamenting about the past.

“If there isn’t a Kitty sitting on your lap tonight, I’ll consider it,” Princess Qian Qian Yu snorted.

“Actually, that was an accident. She was tired from practising,and wanted to go to soak in a hot bath to wash off her exhaustion. However, you should know that there was still a pitiful me who was waiting for a certain heartless Princess that verbally agreed but never made it to an appointment, “Yue Yang smiled as he thought that the face of a jealous Tigress was adorable.

“No matter what, she still sat on your lap,” Princess Qian Qian felt that the process was not important and the result was exactly what she saw.

“You don’t mind the lttle slave, so why do you start overturning your vinegar jar [2] when it comes to Kitty? Didn’t you call her sister? Besides, she was so tired that things were not as they seemed yesterday. I just gave her a massage on her shoulders and her arms; there was absolutely nothing of the sort that you’re thinking about?” Yue Yang felt a little strange. Before he pushed down Drunken Cat Yu Jie, Princess Qian Qian and the others always cheered and helped secretly by trying to make it happen, so why was she unhappy now?

T/L Note: [2] The act of becoming jealous.

“It doesn’t matter if you massaged her shoulders or something; it doesn’t even matter to me if you did anything with her last night— what matters was the fact that you whispered and plotted against a certain pitiful Princess last night. It seems a bit excessive, doesn’t it?” Princess Qian looked at Yue Yang like a judge looking at a thief, eager to see what kind of defences he could muster.

“There is absolutely no conspiracy,” Yue Yang shouted in injustice.

“I’ve heard it with my own ears, how dare you deny it?” Princess Qian Qian implied that the evidence was overwhelming and the culprit must plead guilty.

“Ah, what have you heard?” Yue Yang felt a little hot as he forgot that his Tigress had a talent for her sixth senses.

“For example, at some point, someone would help you out and take down a Princess who has been kept in the dark about the entire thing…” Princess Qian Qian gave Yue Yang a hint as she rolled her eyes coquettishly, “I really don’t know what sort of techniques you used! After a night of being eaten out, the originally bashful and loving Sister Ye instantly turns her back on us and aid in your schemes. You are to blame for this— the wonderful Sister Ye has now been changed by you!”

“Haha, successfully transforming Kitty is an achievement that I will forever be proud of in my life. Tigress, she is not malicious, she is just slightly more benevolent. She just wants to share the good things with you!” Yue Yang laughed wickedly.

“I’ll make you feel pain and pleasure, that’s what I’ll do!” Princess Qian Qian couldn’t bear it any longer and punched the kid away.

What does it mean to ‘take advantage but act innocent’?

This kid was the epitome of that!

***

Feng Ji rushed for three days and became more obedient towards the two ‘bosses’, Yue Yang and Princess Qian Qian. No matter how fast or how far he rode the Dromaeosaur, sometimes the two of them were already dozens of kilometres behind, but in the blink of an eye, they could catch up.

The long-legged Dromaeosaur had not recovered at first, but with sufficient food in the past few days, it had recovered 90% of the speed.

It’s speed was almost the same as flying.

However, the two bosses were willing to hold hands and fly leisurely in the sky, not a second faster nor a metre slower than the other. That kind of flight control could make many Heaven Ranked powerhouses smash their heads to the wall in an act of seeking death. The most terrifying thing was that sometimes, if the Master Titan and the Mistress were interested, they would run off to enjoy the scenery or have a picnic, where they would put a distance of hundreds of kilometres between them but catch up with one teleportation.

Possessing strong strength and learning to teleport was not considered a stupendous feat.

The problem was that due to the long-term melee, all kinds of abnormal energies permeate this Heavenly Splendour Territory. Plus, there were strange space laws in many places, so even their flights might not have turned out smoothly, let alone teleportation.

Besides, at a distance of several hundred kilometres, they were able to find a person’s precisely unparalleled precision. Then, ignoring the influence and constraints of the spatial energy, they instantly teleported.

What kind of control did this require?

Not mentioning any City Lords or Feudal Lords, even if it was the Country Lord, Feng Ji guessed that no one had such capabilities.

Could it be that the Master Titan who hired him was really a powerhouse that surpassed the strength of even the Country Lord? If that was true, then what was a strong man like him doing in the Heavenly Splendour Territory, which was already in a mess? This Master Tita was probably not a powerhouse invited by one of the three Domain Emperors to help out in the war, right?

Feng Ji made assumptions and hypotheses throughout the entire journey. However, curiosity can kill the cat, but the fact remains that there was no answer.

“Hiss!”

With its head held high and its long legs hurriedly running, it suddenly called out.

An enemy appeared? Feng Ji understood the abnormal reaction of his mount. There must be an enemy somewhere in front and the smell of the wind made it uneasy!

There shouldn’t be any Heaven Ranked Demon Beasts lurking nearby that could pose a threat to their safety, so could it be a warrior instead of a Demon Beast?

How could a powerful warrior appear here?

Strictly speaking, they were still within the scope of the Golden Sun City. This was the border intersection of the Golden Sun City, the Yang Ti City and also the three cities of the Red Forest. Mercenaries wouldn’t even come to these poor and slum-filled places if it weren’t for completing missions, let alone thieves and bandits to loot. Because if the band of thieves came here, maybe they would starve to death in such a dire place. So, if it was not a mercenary nor was it a thief, could it be an army?

The problem was that this was the rear instead of a chaotic area. Unless the Commander of the army was insane, it was impossible to drive soldiers to such a place.

Feng Ji hurriedly stopped the long-legged Dramaeosaur and stopped to wait for Yue Yang and Princess Qian Qian.

He was no longer the City Lord, but a guard.

So there was no need for him to make up his mind as his job was to report this kind of troubling matters to the higher-ups.

“Is something wrong? Why did you stop?” Yue Yang was chatting and laughing with Princess Qian Qian before he found that Feng Ji had stopped and stepped forward to ask.

“There seems to be an enemy in front as it smells a threatening scent. My guess is that there are enemies that are at the Heaven Ranks,” Feng Ji himself couldn’t understand why there were Heaven Ranked enemies here, so he was very puzzled.

“Now that you mention it, I can sense something strange as well. There are about less than a thousand people who are coming towards us. The source of maliciously murderous intent is also in that direction. The source of that intent is merely at the Heaven Ranked level? It seems that there are only one or two of them, so that is not considered a threat to be afraid of!” After hearing this, Princess Qian Qian used her Six Records to sense it. First, she looked at things from a distance before wrinkling her nose. Finally shaking her head, she indicated that she couldn’t see them clearly.

“Your Highness, you can see the enemy?” Feng Ji was so frightened that he couldn’t see the enemy even with his cat’s eyes. How could this Mistress, who was a Princess, actually see it?

“It’s not seeing with the eyes; it’s sensing. Forget it, I don’t want to explain,” Princess Qian Qian waved her hand.

In fact, she felt a tinge of regret in her heart despite the powerful ability of the Six Records.

But compared to the Spirit Domain that Xue Wu Xia cultivated in the Gates of Life and Death, there was still a great distance between the two abilities. If Xue Wu Xia held the Book of Truth, she would probably be able to ‘see’ how many enemies they were going up against and how strong they were. It was clear that her senses were much better than her own power of the Six Records. Due to the gap in ability, she was still unable to provide Yue Yang with accurate information.

It seemed that if she wanted to surpass Xue Wu Xia and become his greatest help, she would have to work hard and practice harder!

Princess Qian Qian was lamenting her own inadequacy, but she didn’t realise that her dissatisfaction with herself came off as frightening to Feng Ji when he nearly felt compelled to drop to his knees after listening to her declaration.

For a long time, Feng Ji’s ultimate pride was to have a pair of extraordinary Cat Eyes. These eyes could see farther than anyone else and were the most accurate! Even compared to the Eagle Eyes of the Eagle Clan, his ability was not inferior at all. Unexpectedly, his Mistress actually had a pair of eyes that were a hundred times stronger than his own. Before he could even see the shadows, she could already see the number of enemies and figure out that there were only two powerful Heaven Ranked enemies… What kind of eyes were those?

He really wanted to ask the Mistress about her origin clan. Was she from the legendary Heaven Eye Clan?

That’s not right either!

This princess doesn’t have a third eye at all and she didn’t look like someone from the Heaven Eye Clan!

“A very weak aura mixed with a very violent aura makes things rather puzzling. Well, let’s not guess, let’s go and see!” Yue Yang could also sense them to some extent, but before his Supreme Will was fully expanded, coupled with the fact that he was hiding his powers, his Heaven Eyes Transverse was unable to ‘see as clearly’ as the Six Records of the Princess. Besides, the distance was quite far, so he was a bit indifferent about trying to ‘see’ as well.

“It seems that there are still cries— cries of women and children!” Princess Qian Qian and Yue Yang glanced at each other before they sped up and flw ahead at the same time.

“…I can’t hear anything! We’re dozens of kilometres away and they hear the cries of women and children? This- what kind of hearing is this?” Feng Ji was now completely in admiration. Compared to Master Titan and Her Highness, the Princess, he was really a scumbag. No wonder that Fat Toad back then felt that he didn’t even have the qualifications to be a pathfinder!

chapters.title: Chapter 925
chapters.body:
Chapter 925

Chapter 925: Evil? Evil Deeds Will Be Retributed!

“Hurry up, hurry up! Run, run!”

“If you want to survive, run as fast as you can!”

In the dense forest, nearly 1000 naked and starving people were running wild with bare feet as everyone scrambled to the front. Even the dozens of mercenaries who were wearing shoes and were responsible for maintaining order between the people, were also caught in the mess. The captain of the mercenary group was covered in blood. He was carrying a giant axe as he waved his hand continuously, indicating for the exhausted horde of people to run faster without even stopping for a moment.

Faintly, in the dense forest they left behind, there was a kind of Red Wolf with red eyes and a huge body.

As long as the mercenaries in charge of guarding the people didn’t pay attention to their surroundings, the wolves would jump out of their hiding places and attack at lightning speed. With their huge mouths, they could easily grab the skinny prey and drag them away as food.

Their numbers were as large as 20 to 30.

And they were very cunning.

Naturally, they knew how to deal with mercenaries. The moment any mercenary that was responsible for taking care of the people started to relax briefly, they would start their attack!

“Beast, get out of here!” The mercenary captain was the only one who could fight against this kind of Red Wolf alone, but even his giant axe wasn’t able to kill this kind of greedy and thick-skinned wolf that was blinded with hunger.

A silver axe cut through the air and slashed down.

There was a starving villager that was clamped in the wolf’s mouth. His head and neck were wounded and the skin around his neck was split as well. The man’s blood splashed all over the scene.

It cried out and reluctantly dropped the prey in its mouth. Then, it flattened its tail and quickly fled into the forest.

The starving man who was thrown on the ground was already dead. His head was completely ripped, causing his brain matter to gurgle out. Although his hands and feet were still twitching slightly, the situation was absolutely hopeless. The mercenary captain didn’t need to look at the corpse to know that this Red Wolf was extremely cruel. Even if it had to throw away the food in its mouth, it would kill the enemy first. Unless one’s strength was above the Red Wolf, no one would be able to survive being in the jaws of this canine.

He shook his head as he turned around and waved his arms vigorously, “Run, everyone! Run faster, you’ll run out of the Red Wolf Forest in no time, so run as fast as you can!”

After he ran a few steps away from the scene, the wounded Red Wolf stuck its head out again.

It cunningly observed the situation and in an instant, it dragged the corpse of the once-starving man who gritted his teeth into the bushes. It didn’t give up at all; it was just that the opponent was very strong and could not be matched, so it had to give in a little.

While this Red Wolf was tearing at the body, more Red Wolves were still sauntering behind, looking for opportunities to attack.

Usually there was a shortage of food in the forest. This time, as a large number of prey passed by, they naturally did not want to miss it.

Plop, plop!

More than a dozen vines hanging in the forest caused the hungry citizens, who were running for their lives in a panic, to trip over.

At first, only a few people fell to the ground.

But the people in the back merely followed their lead and didn’t observe the front terrain clearly. Even if they did, they couldn’t hold back their pace. The vines made them trip, and they made their companions trip— becoming a never-ending chain reaction. Except for a few people who still maintained a sliver of strength who were able to cross, the rest of the starving people who stumbled and fled, all fell to the ground like a pile of collapsing dominoes.

When the mercenary captain found out and rushed over to deal with the situation, the ground was already full of the fatigued people.

There were also some people who didn’t stumble and fall, but they were worried that they would fall into the wolves’ jaws if they left the team.

They dared not run any more, so they stayed by their companions in fear.

Even the group of people who ran past the area where the vines had already ended started to look back. Even upon seeing this situation, they didn’t know what to do. Should they continue to escape or go back? If they continued to run away, then a few people would not be enough to fight the wolves. If they went back to the team, they might be safe for the time being, but there were definitely even more terrifying creatures lurking behind the wolves! If those enemies were able to catch up to them, it would definitely be more tragic than dying in the mouths of the wolves. Now, what should they do? Keep running, or stop and take a breath?

“Fools! If you don’t run fast and allow the enemy to catch up, it will be over!” A few daring but hungry people spread their feet and continued to move forward.

They were all men. Although they were thin, they were still men. Hence, they were better than women in terms of decision-making.

T/L Note: Ouch author, I feel insulted. Thanks?

Among the thousands of people, most of them were the elderly, the weak, the women and the children.

They couldn’t make a decision at all, so they could only look at the mercenary captain who was in charge of everyone’s safety. They hoped that he could come up with a good idea!

“Ah!!!” Several men that ran in front were thrown down by the Red Wolves who circled to the front as they screamed in pain.

The situation shocked the entire team.

There were wolves in front of them as well; what should they do?

“Run, and don’t care about the wolves. We’ll deal with them, so just keep running! The real enemy is searching for us, and if they catch up, all of us will die!” The mercenary captain ordered five team members to quickly catch up and protect the front crowd. He yelled anxiously, signalling the people to not stop for even a second, let alone rest. They must run with all their strength; otherwise, they would not be able to survive at all.

“Save me, save me…”

An old man was knocked over by a wolf as he grabbed a vine and cried out for help.

The Red Wolf pulled hard, but it couldn’t move the man. Annoyed, it tore the old man’s body in half with his front paws.

The old man hadn’t died yet, but half of his body fell to the ground. Rolling over and over, his final whimpers were utterly tragic. The mercenary captain closed his eyes— he had seen too many lives gone in such scenarios.

When they set off, there were nearly 10,000 people fleeing for their lives.

Now, there were less than a thousand people left.

He waved his hand vigorously and shouted loudly, “Everyone can huddle closer, but you must keep moving forward, I will not wait for those who stay, so if any of you can’t go forward, then I will definitely leave you behind. If you want to survive, then run as fast as you can. This place is not far from the Golden Sun City. If you run there, everyone will be able to survive. You have all run thousands of kilometres already, do you want to just give up here? Those who are physically fit, take the children; for those who have no strength, grit your teeth and hold on! We just need to pass the Red Wolf Forest and there are almost no beasts behind anymore. If we get to a safe place, we will rest for a while, but now is not the time for a break!”

“Hold him! Hua Er, remember to take good care of your brother, understand?” An old woman suddenly handed the baby in her arms to the thin little girl beside her.

“Grandma…” The little girl trembled and shivered, but her little blackened hands tightly hugged the baby.

“You’re a big girl already, so you need to be sensible and take care of your younger brother. Grandma can’t walk anymore, so you have to live well in the future,” The old woman smiled before she leaned towards the little girl and kissed her on the forehead lightly, “I My dear, you must live; the merciful gods will take care of you from the sky. At any time, don’t give up hope! The hope of our family falls on you, so you have to work hard to live, Hua Er!”

The little girl burst into tears and nodded desperately.

She matured early, which was why she knew what was going to happen next.

It was not only one old woman who resorted to such desperate measures, but dozens of old people entrusted the baby in their arms or their relatives to others, before staying behind themselves to be fed to the wolves with their bodies.

As in previous adventures, through dangerous areas infested by ferocious beasts, sacrifices must be made to allow the rest to escape.

There were only 20 or 30 Red Wolves, which was a relatively small number.

A few dozen people could fill their bellies, at least, to the point where they would temporarily stop chasing the fleeing crowd and concentrate on feasting on the blood and meat. Seeing a group of hungry people stay behind, the mercenary captain did not refuse or retort. In fact, he had no better way— this sacrifice was already the best way! He didn’t have a strong ability as he couldn’t even kill a Red Wolf, so he wasn’t capable of stopping this kind of tragedy from happening at all.

Along the way, he didn’t know how many people volunteered and sacrificed for their loved ones— an act of exchanging their lives for the survivors.

Because of the lack of adult men, it was the wounded who stayed behind at the beginning.

Then, the elderly without relatives volunteered.

After that, no one could really be sacrificed, but there were still people who stood up and gave themselves up. In the entire team, the ones with the highest survival rate were the babies and the little girl because they were the last hope of the entire fleeing team.

If they died as well, there would be no point in fleeing!

“Grandma’s got to go!” The old woman took out a dagger as the Red Wolf, who probed into the distance, approached with all its strength to pounce on the woman. When the Red Wolf wanted to knock her onto the ground with one claw, she raised her last strength and stabbed it in the eye with a dagger. Then, she straightened her limbs with a smile. On her chest and abdomen, a deep claw mark smashed all the internal organs.

She had never thought of successfully killing a Red Wolf, but she hoped that she could do her last bit in exchange for her granddaughter and grandson’s escape.

Of the dozens of starving people who remained to sacrifice themselves, very few of them succeeded in injuring the Red Wolves.

On the contrary, these people allowed the Red Wolves to hunt them as they were torn to shreds.

The fleeing crowd, with tears in their eyes, hit the road again. In the middle of the crowd, the little girl’s dirty face was cleansed with the white trails of her tears, but she didn’t allow herself to cry. The girl gritted her teeth desperately and hugged her brother tightly as she staggered but continued to run amidst the crowd.

She didn’t know how long this escape would last, but her survival instincts made her go beyond her limits again and again.

“While the Red Wolf is eating, we must leave the Red Wolf Forest as soon as possible. We will be safe once we get to the City, so we have to hurry up and run!” The mercenary captain was very anxious— the truly terrifying enemy were not the Red Wolves, but an existence that was a hundred times more terrifying than a Red Wolf. If those enemies caught up with them, the entire crowd would definitely be wiped out as it was impossible to pass by because the tactic of leaving a few hundred people wouldn’t work.

“How touching, I almost cried,” A sharp voice came from the depths of the forest.

“That was a good parting of life and death; I too, appreciate this play very much. If only a few more people could die, it would be a little more touching!” Another deep voice sounded as the timbre of the voice resembled muffled thunder.

Hearing this voice, all the hungry people who were desperately fleeing felt their knees go weak.

In an even more exaggerated scene than stumbling over the vines, one by one, the people fell to the ground as they trembled with fear.

In panic, their eyes reflected a fear that was more stricken than seeing the supernatural!

Before even mentioning the states of the old, weak women and the children, even the mercenary captain who had been calm the entire time, felt his face become ashen.

It turned out that dozens of mercenaries, who had been protecting the fleeing and starved people, were dumbfounded. Some weapons fell off their hands and some even knelt on their knees to beg for mercy. A few mercenaries left the hungry people and tried their best to escape, but a few leaves of the surrounding trees snapped off and flew like darts, passing through the necks of the fleeing mercenaries in a rush.

When those mercenaries fell back to the ground, they were already dead.

In a split second!

They did not even have the strength to resist— just a few leaves could instantly kill these mercenaries who had the strengths of 5th Level Earth Rankers.

“I’m so disappointed. I thought there would be a few people who would rush to resist and make great self-sacrifices, but I didn’t expect there to be no one,” The man, who sounded like a night owl, floated out in the form that was similar to a bat. Seeing this person appear, the greedy Red Wolves were also frightened as they scattered. They didn’t care to snatch the flesh and blood on the ground any more as they fled frantically with their tails between their paws.

Of course, their results were the same as a few fleeing mercenaries.

They couldn’t see the strange man taking action at all. It seemed that there was a whirlwind around him as dozens of fallen leaves flew out, killing the Red Wolves that even the mercenary captain couldn’t, in a split second.

The Red Wolves’ entire bodies were slashed without mercy.

What seemed to be predators turned into corpses at this moment!

The mercenary captain was desperate. This man’s strength was stronger and weirder than the rumours that circulated, so he didn’t have any chance at all!

He sighed, raising the giant axe in his hand and prepared to kill himself. He knew that if he fell into the hands of this man, he would definitely regret living in this world. Rather than living only to yearn for the embrace of death, it would be better to end it early and not suffer from that torture!

“I don’t remember agreeing to allow you to die? Do you think my ‘Hand of Manipulation’ is just a fluke? My Hand of Manipulation can not only manipulate the life of the enemy, but also the death of the enemy,” A strange man with the shape of a night owl and a bat flicked his finger. Soon, a piece of fallen leaf floated up silently, cutting the giant axe in the hands of the mercenary captain in half.

“You have all fled so far— if you give up like this, it will be way too boring, so let me play a game with you. I’m going to count to 100, so if any of you can escape my sight, then this City Lord will let you go. So stand up and run away, all of you! Hahaha, how merciful I am!” The man who had a loud voice that sounded like a broken gong appeared. Out revealed a noble man who was jointly carried by the shoulders of 18 slaves as he sat on the golden throne. Dressed in a set of treasured armour and holding a glass of wine, his blood-red cloak was ruffling against the wind. Between his knees, there was even a naked slave girl who poured his wine and she remained by his feet.

“…” Seeing this man appear, the hearts of all the fugitives felt entirely desolated.

This man who claimed to be very kind was ten times more vicious than the guy who liked to manipulate other people’s lives and death just now. No one would object if he were to be named as the most cruel and vile person in the entire Heavenly Splendour Territory.

No one made a move because everyone knew what the outcome was.

It’s no use running away.

Not to mention the fact of being able to get out of his sight after a hundred counts; even if they could, this bastard would never keep his word!

There was a dead silence in the crowd, only the little girl holding her brother, bowed her head and squeezed out of the crowd as she rushed forward desperately. “Yes, yes, that’s it! Run faster, faster! I’ll start counting soon, don’t you all want to survive like her? I’ll give you this chance! Everyone, run away! I’m only counting 1 now; pay attention, I’m only counting 1 now! When I count to one hundred, whoever among you can escape out of my sight, I will set you free… How pathetic, none of you responded to my proposal! This City Lord is too annoyed; can’t you guys struggle a bit to give me pleasure? Fortunately, there is still a little girl participating in my game, haha! Yes, I really like people who always hold on to hope in their hearts. It is such a gloriously happy thing to kill the hope in someone’s heart. Well, it’s time to count! 2… 3, 4, 5, 6, little girl, you have to run faster, faster, haha! Next, after that is 7, 8, 9? And after that? Of course, it is 100! Well, little girl, you know my maths is not very good, so it’s a pity that you haven’t run out of sight when I counted to 100— you should have been faster!”

“Ah, ah, let go!” In a flash, a slave grabbed the little girl and threw her under the golden throne like a cooped up chicken.

The starving citizens hid their faces and couldn’t bear to look up.

They knew this guy couldn’t be trusted, but so what? Without power, there was no right or wrong!

The little girl cried as she hugged her younger brother tightly and sobbed, “9 is followed by 10, not 100…” Even now, she remains serious. Although she was sensible, she was but a child after all; she didn’t understand why the other party would break a promise just like that.

The middle-aged man sitting on the golden seat laughed so hard that tears were splashing out.

He patted the armrest of the throne, “Yes, 10 comes after 9 and not 100, but I like to call 100 in advance, can’t I? I didn’t say that I must count them all, hahaha! Do you think this City Lord is particularly hateful? Do you feel particularly aggrieved? Be a little more sad or be a little more angry; I like to see your eyes that dare not speak out! Haha, poor little bastards, pathetic… Seeing you all helpless is my greatest pleasure as a City Lord. You will never understand; this is a kind of fulfilment that is unique to the strong. But what is the strong? It means that you can’t dominate and manipulate the fate of everyone else!”

“Do you still want to play?” The strange man with the voice of a night owl and the shape of a bat almost frowned as his eyebrows furrowed.

“Don’t be nervous, no one will come here. Besides, the Country Lord has long since abandoned this area and it has become an abandoned place. Who’ll give a damn even if these weak ants die? It’s rare for us to come out for a breather anyways, so going back immediately without having some fun is too much of a wasted chance. You should know that it has been so long since anyone dared to make a break for it!” The middle-aged man sitting on the golden seat laughed loudly.

“Then, do as you like…” The man with a voice like a night owl and the shape of a bat shook his head as he fluttered to the side.

“Men, peel off this little girl’s skin first. But be careful, don’t damage it because it must be intact. As for the baby, it’s too thin and there’s nothing to eat. Hmm, dig out his heart and bake it. Also, I want to have some sweet and delicious brain fluids, don’t make them salty like the last time,” The middle-aged man sitting on the golden seat gave orders at will.

“Don’t kill me! Please, spare us!” The little girl was so frightened that she slumped on the ground. A slave approached her directly, wanting to dig the baby’s heart. The little girl screamed and pounced on the slave as she opened her mouth, her only weapon, and gnawed at the enemy with her teeth.

This was her only resistance.

Although it was futile, it was a form of resistance after all, unlike the starved people and mercenaries who waited numbly to die.

The mercenary captain secretly took out a dagger, but before he could commit suicide, he found that his entire body was paralyzed and he was standing stiffly, unable to blink.

Sure enough, in front of the enemy who has the ability to ‘manipulate with his hands’, it was impossible to commit suicide. If he couldn’t die, then his ending might not be better than that of the skinned little girl… It’s over, everything is over!

“I appreciate this kind of resistance. If everyone behaved as such, then this CIty Lord wouldn’t be this bored. Before peeling off her skin, let’s pry off her teeth one by one. Note that you shouldn’t break the roots of her teeth. You must pry it out in one piece and make sure it’s intact. I repeat, make sure it is intact because I want to save it for my collection!” The middle-aged man sitting on the golden seat drank a glass of wine with satisfaction as he made up his mind— first, he would pull out her teeth and then skin her; that way, he would be able to have two forms of collectibles.

Just when the slave took out her sharp knife to pull out the girl’s teeth, the slave’s body shook slightly.

And then, she suddenly fell to the ground.

After a few seconds, the body split into two halves from top to the bottom.

“What’s happened?” The middle-aged man sitting on the golden seat was stunned. The wine in his hand was spilled— who did it? Who was it that was capable of killing someone in front of him, yet still managed to conceal themselves? The kill was so quick and stealthy, and it left not a trace of a clue!

A shadow flew past from afar, landing beside the little girl in plain sight as the figure reached out to hug the little girl, “Fortunately, she’s not hurt. This damned place is so annoying, I can’t believe teleportation didn’t work! Idiot, we nearly couldn’t make it. Why are you still standing there? Move it and kill every single one of them! No, actually, take them down first but pry out their teeth! I’m so angry!!”

The person who came was, of course, Princess Qian Qian, and the angry Tigress was definitely not to be provoked.

Now, it could be said that she was very angry!

The shadow that appeared behind her was not Yue Yang. Instead, it was Feng Ji, the poor thief who claimed to be unparalleled in speed but couldn’t keep up with Princess Qian Qian’s flight.

As for Yue Yang, he had already sat on the back of the golden seat at some point. It didn’t matter to him if he sat on the back of someone else’s chair; he even stretched his legs and directly placed his big foot on the shoulder of the middle-aged man, “You used a Space-Imprisoning treasure, didn’t you? In my entire life, this is the first time that this Young Master arrived late, so how are you going to compensate for that? Teeth and human skin are useless as the noble taste of this Young Master is not something that can be directly compared with a piece of garbage like you!”

“What?” The middle-aged man was immediately enraged. Someone dared to step on his shoulders? They’re just asking for death!

He jumped up with all his strength and lunged at Yue Yang.

chapters.title: Chapter 926
chapters.body:
Chapter 926

Chapter 926: A Heavy Figurative Slap In The Face

The one who reacted the fastest was not the middle-aged man who let Yue Yang step on his shoulders, but the strange man whose voice was like a night owl as he flew in the shape of a bat.

He stretched out his hand.

A grotesque breeze swirled around him and several fallen leaves immediately accelerated. The scene was similar to summoning a beast as the leaves flew towards Yue Yang. But the Hand of Manipulation, which had never failed, suddenly failed. Just like a marionette, the wires connecting to the ‘puppet’ were cut by scissors and lost control. The fallen leaves that flew halfway all fluttered in the wind without a trace of strength. The strange man who stretched out his hand to control the life and death of others, was very stunned as his facial expressions morphed into an emotion of shock, as if he was actually punched in the face before realising.

At the same time, there was a layer of humiliation on his face that only appeared after being insulted.

He stared at Yue Yang stubbornly.

The middle-aged man, who claimed to be the City Lord, remained seated on the golden seat, but his eyes were also bursting with anger and his fists slammed at Yue Yang viciously.

“Welcome to my game! I promise, you will be very happy!” If the man actually managed to land a hit on Yue Yang, he wouldn’t call himself the Third Young Master that killed the Demon King and Red Sky River. If he was serious, he could completely kill the enemy in seconds, but Yue Yang did not do that. That was because he wanted to give this enemy, who liked to use human lives to play games, a surprise!

If student Yue Yang were to rank all the specialties of his life, the enviable talent of picking up girls would probably not be ranked first.

But if there was one speciality, if placed second, he guessed that his other special tricks and tactics wouldn’t be able to win first place over it either.

That specialty was abuse.

Yue Yang was not Buddha— he was thick-skinned, had a smart brain and w.as actually rather intelligent Therefore, the unique student Yue Yang was much faster and adequate in playing his role of dealing with his enemies. Through countless experiences and battles, the transmigrated man has grown and learnt immensely, gradually nurturing his own form of individuality and style. Currently, his motto was, ‘a tooth for a tooth, an eye for an eye’.

Many of the great people could do something like being slapped on the left cheek and also shoving their right cheek to be slapped once more—repaying grievances with virtue and influencing the enemy; many people who are not very great but were broad-minded could also be magnanimous and lenient to an extent. Even if a dog bit a person, the person would not bite the dog back; at most, they would take a detour and definitely choose not to have the same perspective as a dog! [1]

T/L Note:[1] The above paragraph explains 2 types of people when they’re hurt or backstabbed. The 1st ones are like saints, who empathise and try to reform the bad guys. The 2nd type wouldn’t be that nice, but would attempt to avoid them and choose not to do the same towards them because they know they’re better than that.

Student Yue Yang naturally knew about these principles.

But knowing about it, didn’t mean that he had to do it.

According to Yue Yang’s style of doing things, if someone slapped the left side of his face once, he would slap the other person ten times. If he remained unsatisfied, he would chop off the person’s hand. But sometimes, things might be different. If a dog bit him once, Yue Yang would definitely choose not to biteback. Instead, he would have severed the dog’s head immediately.

After all, in Yue Yang’s heart, there were some differences between a dog and a human.

Just now, the middle-aged man who liked to play games very much, had lucked out because he met Yue Yang, who had decided to play with him.

Playing games, right?

Who wouldn’t!

“Actually, I don’t really like to eat roasted hearts and livers; sweet brains are also just so-so for me. But, seeing as it is a local delicacy,I would have to try it anyway. Don’t worry, after eating this meal, I will promote it to my companions next time. If they do want to try some as well, I will introduce your relatives and friends to them. I’m going to presume that your relatives and friends are as hospitable and open-minded as you are, by the way,” Yue Yang cleverly dodged the middle-aged man’s heavy punches in mid-air. Then, he landed casually and very smugly on the end of the golden chair.

The middle-aged man didn’t even manage to hit him and became even more angry as his voice roared like thunder.

He leapt into the sky, emanating the greatest strengths that he could muster in his body.

The shockwave that he emitted shook the entire Red Wolf Forest, forcing the substantive momentum to descend upon them like a mountain.

The hungry people on the ground were almost suffocated. If they hadn’t hugged each other tightly and used the collective strength to resist, with the strength of a single person, they would have been pressed into meat patties long ago under this terrifying influx of power. As if the middle-aged man was about to destroy the entire earth, he swooped down from a height of several hundred metres in mid-air and slammed down with a heavy punch. His body and fists were still in the air, but all of the starved had been oppressed to the point where they were starting to vomit blood. If it weren’t for Princess Qian Qian, who was holding the little girl and the baby, dodging around the people and raising the power of the Domain, the terrified mercenaries and the other civilians would have been suppressed into meat smithereens due to the shockwave.

The punch was aimed at the centre of the golden seat as the man rammed the entire seat onto the ground.

The seventeen slaves who originally carried the golden seat suffered snapped tendons and broken bones. During the screams, all seven holes [1] bled out before they finally exploded and died, turning into a cloud of blood.

T/L Note: [1] Refers to the eyes, the nostrils, the mouth and the ears.

On the contrary, the female slave who responded quickly escaped from the centre attack as her naked body fell into a pile of sand.

Despite seeing that his fist was about to hit him, Yue Yang remained seated in a very calm manner on the back of the chair.

It was as if he couldn’t see anyone attacking him at all.

Xiao Wen Li flashed out.

Her beautiful big eyes glared at the man.

The heavy punch that was just a metre away from hitting Yue Yang’s head, together with the entire body of the middle-aged man, instantly stagnated in mid-air.

The strange man with a voice like a night owl and a shape like a bat saw this scene and was stunned; he couldn’t believe his eyes. He was very aware of the strength of his companions. Even if he possessed the Hand of Manipulation, he wasn’t stronger than the middle-aged man. However, despite his powerful companion taking aim with his entire power, the opponent was able to freeze his movements with just her eyes?

This kind of talent was too scary, right? Could it be that this little snake loli is… Damn it, she’s not a normal snake demon; she was a Divine Beast!

The strange man who found out that the other party had a Divine Beast was so frightened that he immediately wanted to run away.

What a joke!

No amount of lives were enough to fight against a Divine Beast!

The man with the voice of a night owl and the shape of a bat turned around. He prepared to increase his speed with all his strength to escape from the Red Wolf Forest.

However, behind him, an Ice Serpent Demon was already holding onto a war blade and glaring at him coldly. The strange man in the shape of a bat turned around again, trying to escape from the sides. But when he did so, he realised that there was another Thunderbolt Naga on this side. He looked around and found himself surrounded as the Stone Element Medusa and the Storm Mermaid enclosed him from the other two sides as well.

These powerful creatures had arrived unknowingly— so quickly and silently that even the manipulator of the area was unaware of their arrival.

He was going to die if he didn’t try to escape!

The strange man in the shape of a bat seemed to know what would happen next.

If four of such powerful beings were to strike together, even if he was lucky enough to survive, he would definitely fall to the ground with serious injuries.

Escape— he must escape from here as soon as possible. The strange man in the shape of a bat, with all his strength, soared into the sky desperately. Trying to rush out of the encirclement, he wanted to escape to a safe place thousands of miles away from where he was. In order to survive, he didn’t care about anything. As for his companion, he opted to let the guy die there and then. If it wasn’t for his companion who angered the strong with his wild and boastful words, he wouldn’t have been dragged into this pile of mess either!

Boom!

In the skies above, a barrel-thick flash of thunderbolt exploded, striking the top of the strange man’s head directly.

The strange male who was charred black fell like a meteor and smashed to the ground.

The terrifying thunderbolt, in addition to the bombarding force that almost smashed his head, caused him to be blackened into a shade of coal as he twitched furiously from the electric shock and the concussion.

“Ahhh…” The strange man in the shape of a bat was barely able to move a finger, but a light breeze like the strength of a faint breath, blew silently by his side. The seemingly invisible rope was thrown from the distance as it slid around the strange man’s body. Retrieving him forcefully, the blackened body of the strange man was pulled into the distance.

“Whoosh!”

A sharp arrow was pinned to the back of the creepy man’s wriggling hand without any mistake; this was the masterpiece of the Stone Element Medusa.

His hand instantly turned grey and was petrified.

Simultaneously, the effect spread rapidly, extending up his arms and elbows.

On the other hand, the Hand of Manipulation, which no one had seen before, was revealed. The power of petrification did not only turn the strange man’s hand into stone, it also revealed his abilities.

Five thin ‘lines’ appeared on the strange man’s hands, extending to the distant forest.

These originally intangible existences become a stone thread under the prowess of the Stone Element Medusa’s petrification ability!

As for the middle-aged man who attacked Yue Yang, he was still stagnant in the air, unable to move at all! Red Sky River, who had a Divine Body, could only restrain Xiao Wen Li for a mere second. Hence, wanting to win against some nobody City Lord with a Divine Beast of restraining abilities was simply easier than eating beans! If she wanted to, she could freeze him for two or three hours. Student Yue Yang ignored the guy above his head, but criticised the weird man who wanted to run away with a bit of anger, “You disappoint me too much. You- how can you behave like this? If you want to run away, just say so, how can you run away secretly?”

When everyone heard it, they sweated profusely.

Unless they were supposed to exclaim to the world and say, “Hey, pay attention guys, I’m going to run away!”

The strange man in the shape of a bat was already desperate, but now he felt a little bit of vitality surging within his veins. Could it be that the other party wanted to release him?

Yue Yang’s expression was very serious and he raised a finger in a serious manner, “Actually, if you want to run away, it is not forbidden. On the contrary, I encourage running away! Let’s play the game just now! Feng Ji, you can do the honours. Count to 100 and be careful, don’t miss a number; you must count standardly. If you can escape my sight by the time he counts to 100, you can be free!”

He instructed Feng Ji to count forward and snapped his fingers.

It turned out that the middle-aged man, whose body was still in the air, slammed onto the ground with the snap of his fingers. The shocking force almost destroyed a large area of the surrounding forest.

However, no one was shocked by the terrifying power wrought by the middle-aged man.

Everyone was beginning to understand what the two demons, the strange man and the middle-aged man, would face next. They were going to face a more terrifying punishment. A strong man who liked to give an eye for an eye was preparing to carry out a heart-warming punishment! The game that was originally proposed by the enemy was backfiring them as it also brought on bouts of humiliation that their words were used against themselves.

“Boss, I’m counting? Can I?” Feng Ji was a little excited, but also slightly uneasy. What should he do if he messed up?

“If you can’t, I’ll fire you!” Yue Yang always forbade his subordinates to say no.

“Don’t, I’ll just count okay? I’m really going to start…” He raised his head to look at Princess Qian Qian as he had already realised that this Mistress had the right to speak. The Master Titan truly favoured her as other wives of Innate powerhouses would never be able to do so.

“What a lot of nonsense!” Although Princess Qian Qian often fought with Yue Yang in private, she never allowed anyone to question him outside.

“Run away. If you don’t run away, you will die. If you don’t want your teeth and a piece of skin to become my collection, then start running away now! This merciful Young Master is giving you this opportunity! I forgot to tell you, I very much agree with what you said just now. Poor bastards, ravaging you would be the greatest joy of this Young Master. Seeing you weak and tortured to death at my feet makes me feel very fulfilled. You will never understand, but this kind of achievement was unique only to the strong. What is the meaning of ‘strong’, you might ask? They are the ones who can control the fate of others at will!”

Yue Yang retorted the middle-aged man’s words without reservation.

This kind of metaphorical slap in the face was more uncomfortable than death!

The problem was that the middle-aged and the weird man have no possibilities to refute Yue Yang.

Just like the gap between the starved and the duo, although their strengths were of the Heaven Rank, compared to Yue Yang, they were also powerless to resist. No matter what Yue Yang wanted to do to them, they could only wait for their deaths.

“…” The strange man knelt down weakly on the ground while the middle-aged man, whose face was full of ashes, started trembling all over. Now, they began to feel the despair of the hungry people just now and began to understand the fear in the hearts of the ‘ants’ they killed before. They realised how pitiful it was to be abandoned by fate. If they could turn back, they would definitely not chase these fugitives, nor would they even happily kill those poor worms who were crawling under their feet begging for mercy! If they could turn back time, they would regret everything they have done today and everything before. However, there was no possibility of a re-do in this world!

“Cough, cough!” The middle-aged man’s heart, which was originally elated, suddenly filled with fear as he vomited.

“I’m counting; I’m really counting, 1, 2, 3…” Feng Zhi was afraid that Yue Yang, the boss, would be angry, so he quickly counted.

No joke, he was still a temporary guard after all. If he didn’t do well, would there be good rewards to have later on?

Besides, it’s not wrong to punish and hurt these two notorious guys!

The wicked need the wicked to beat them senseless!

The moment these two guys met the villain, Master Titan, this was what they deserved. He guessed that they have probably sinned too many times and cursed many innocent lives, so the retribution was nowserved!

The trembling strangeman suddenly raised his hand to his neck— he wanted to commit suicide!

A few leaves instantly turned into blades.

But the moment when it touched his neck, Yue Yang snapped his fingers. Those blade-like leaves burned and turned into a light whiff of smoke, disappearing without a trace. The strange man’s skin was as perfect as ever; not even a trace of a scar was seen.

He wanted to die, but the decision was no longer in his hands.

“Do you think you can commit suicide without my consent? Do you think my name ‘Master Titan’ is all for nothing? This Master’s Titan Hand can not only manipulate the life of the enemy, but also the death of the enemy!” Student Yue Yang crossed his waist with great arrogance, just like doing akimbo training, as he proudly returned all the strange man’s words.

A figurative slap being able to damage one to this extent was absolutely effective.

Of course, this was just the beginning. If Yue Yang could make the enemy vomit, he would never relent until the enemy cries out of agony!

chapters.title: Chapter 927
chapters.body:
Chapter 927

Chapter 927: Your Speed Is Quite Good, Ain’t It?

The Golden Sun City had an additional batch of refugees, whereas the Hei Ling City welcomed an additional group of guests.

These guests were a little strange.

They were different.

In front of them was a sharp-eyed pathfinder thief that rode a Dromaeosaur. There were dozens of mercenaries in the middle of the team, holding a dozen criminals who had been tortured and had their flesh cut off and teeth shattered. The captives’ bodies almost turned into skeletons as they were bound in the prison cart, gradually wheeled into town. At the end of the line, there was even a huge ‘big-footed ground dragon’. Along the road covered with bones, the dragon walked slowly while it carried a luxurious golden seat on its back.

On the golden throne, in addition to two silver-armored warriors dressed alike each other, there was also a female slave and a little girl holding a baby.

Unlike ordinary caravans, this team resembled a slave-catching team.

But it was rare to see that a slave-catching team flaunt as luxuriously as they did. They obtained a quasi-heaven Ranked ‘big-footed ground dragon’ that was usually only qualified to be steered by a City Lord or a higher rank, even getting a golden throne that was incomparably precious and conspicuous. The most surprising part was that there was not a single noble person with a particularly distinguished status in sight; only two warriors, a lowly female slave and a little girl who looked like a starving person. Even if they were sitting at the feet of the two silver-armoured warriors, sitting on the incomparably luxurious golden throne while looking down at their surroundings did nothing to mask the timidity and the anxiousness in their eyes. Anyone with a little discernment could tell at a glance that they were definitely not nobles. On the contrary, these should be outcasts— the starved among the outcasts!

When did a mere outcast obtain the qualifications to sit on the golden throne?

The Captain of the guard, who was in charge of collecting taxes at the city gate, was surprised by this.

“The stench of wine and meat is evident, yet the road is full of frozen bones [1] …” He vaguely heard the silver-armoured warrior on the left side of the golden seat say this to the silver-armoured warrior on the right.

T/L Note:[1] Means the wealthy or the people in charge can watch others suffer while they enjoy in abundance. The idiom here is very sarcastic but written in a beautiful way.

“Don’t drop idioms here, now is not the time to be hung up on emotions. We have to hurry up and do our business because we don’t have much time. We still have to pass through the Wind-Stopping Swamp. Let’s hurry up and take out the outer ones,” The Captain of the guard at the city gates heard the silver one on the right speak again. Although he could not hear it clearly as the distance was a bit far and their surroundings were a bit noisy, he deduced that the one who just spoke was a woman based on her tone of voice.

“What is your business here?” The guard stepped forward to stop the pathfinder thief walking at the forefront as he shouted loudly. No matter the status or rank, if anyone ever wanted to gain entry into the Hei Ling City, tax evasion was not possible; it was a rule set up by the Feudal Lord.

The Feudal Lord in the Hei Ling City was the younger brother of the Country Lord.

They didn’t have to mention the Hei Ling Territory. Even the entire Zhi Feng Country would not be able to deny the will of the Feudal Lord.

Here, the Feudal Lord wielded the power of command; no matter who it was, as long as one remained in the boundaries of the Hei Ling Territory, there was only one ruler!

The bright-eyed pathfinder thief turned down the back Dromaeosaur with a smile on his face, as if an old friend had not seen him for a long time. He came forward to say hello, but very skillfully stuffed a small purse to the guard and put it in the guard’s hands as he whispered a few things into his ears. He pointed back to the ground dragon and said something in a low voice. In the tax payment with a smile… This was all normal as almost all the caravans or slave-catching teams do this.

Bribing the guards was the most common action of every pathfinder thief, but paying taxes was also in line with the laws of each region.

But this originally bland exchange now made the Captain of the guard twitch nervously.

He didn’t know what he felt, but it was uncomfortable.

The Captain of the guard felt that the pathfinder thief’s laugh was not one of flattery, but an indescribable mockery.

As if he was indicating that the guard who took the money would be led to an unlucky fate very soon… But there was no problem with the gold coins. When the Captain of the guard watched the guards open the purse to register the tax, he found that the gold coins were real. There was no deception and the number was large, far exceeding ordinary slave hunters and even caravans of a larger scale.

What? Did these people earn that much money that they have nowhere to squander it?

Look at the fugitive slaves they captured, all of them were tortured to the point where they couldn’t live any longer; they were completely worthless. Was it really worth paying nearly a thousand gold coins in taxes for these fugitive slaves? Besides, it would already be hard for the other teams to pay a hundred gold as tax, but this pathfinder thief paid nearly a thousand gold, with a face full of smiles.

It was so weird!

When the team entered the city, the Captain of the guard still couldn’t understand why he felt uneasy in his heart?

Obviously, I have received a heavy tax and I have more shares, so I should be happy. So why do I still feel uncomfortable? What is the problem?

“Greyhound, what did he say just now?” the Captain of the guard called his subordinate over to ask him.

“Head, that’s a slave-hunting team that hunts down fugitive slaves. They have travelled between Zhi Feng Country and the Hurricane Country for a long time and this time, they are just passing by. It is said that they are usually mainly responsible for smuggling, so the slaves are only a part-time gig. I guess they obtained a large amount of goods this time as their offer to pay more than 800 yuan in taxes was surprising. Boss, we’re going to have so much fun this month! Even after cutting the highest limit of taxes at 200 yuan, us brothers can finally have a good celebration with the remaining 600! In this poor and rotten place, how long has it been since we received such income. Even working for another half a month, no, half a year, wouldn’t even guarantee us such high raises!” The guard danced with excitement and smiled as he reported the good news to the Captain.

The Captain of the guard nodded, indicating that he knew sd well.

Although there was a chance to line his private pockets, he was not happy. There was even a kind of fear that remained floating in the shadows of his mind.

Impossible… This slave-hunting team would pay 800 gold as tax? Even if smuggling made things easy to earn, it was impossible to pay such taxes when entering the city as it was simply the behaviour of a fool. Who would throw money at others for no reason?

If this slave-hunting team did this on purpose, what was it for?

Why did they bribe their way into the city with a lot of money?

How could that pathfinder thief reveal a mocking smile in the depths of his eyes? How could the lowly female slave and the starving little girl have the right to sit on the golden seat? The most puzzling thing was that the two silver-armoured warriors dressed in the same clothes and seemed obviously very weak, yet how could they be capable of making me feel frightened?

The Captain of the guard in charge of taxation at the entrance of the city couldn’t understand his own thoughts.

After a moment.

In the faraway distance, a griffin flew up to him, carrying a flying messenger covered in blood on its back. Before he could enter the city, the messenger fell down.

When the Captain of the guard rushed forward to rescue the flying messenger, he found that he had been seriously injured and was dying as his final breaths were weak. At the last moment of his death, the flying messenger regained some of his energy and held the hand of the Captain of the guard tightly, “Hurry up, hurry up and report to the Feudal Lord. Yang Ti City, Sha Yan City, Ge Bi City, the City of Thorns, Hu Tao City— they have been destroyed and conquered. I just escaped from Buffalo City… I- I, I was the only one who escaped… Please inform the Feudal Lord and let him know that… Please plead to the Country Lord as well, ahh…”

The messenger seemed to have something to say, but he couldn’t hold it any longer.

With a tilt of his head, he died as he failed to take another breath.

“Immediately, I will…” When the Captain of the guard heard the news, he jumped up immediately— the entire Hei Ling Territory was breached. He didn’t know who it was, but the territory of the non-war zone had also been invaded. Did the war spread to the rear of the Wind-Stopping Swamp area already?

He was preparing to use the fastest speed to rush to the Feudal Lord’s mansion to report the bad news to the Feudal Lord of Hei Ling.

But the fugitive slaves who were bound to the prison carts and the bloody horror of their tortured forms flashed through the Captain of the guard’s head.

Yang Ti City, Sha Yan City, Ge Bi City, City of Thorns, Hu Tao City, and Buffalo City were all overrun by the enemy, so were the City Lords and Garrison Generals all captured?

Could it be… theCaptain of the guard suddenly shivered in fear.

He remembered the face of the pathfinder thief who paid the heavy amounts of tax and still had a smile on his face, the outcast who had no explanation although she sat on the throne, the fugitive slaves who were tortured in their prison carts and the conversation between the similarly dressed man and woman. The Captain of the guard trembled with fright and sweat soaked his whole body like it started to rain.

“What’s the matter? Captain?” Several guards looked at the captain inexplicably.

“I have something very important to report to the Country Lord, but you guys should stay here. By the way, I don’t want the share of the tax; you lot can just split it amongst yourselves! Remember to have a good time at night!” The Captain of the guard wiped his sweat and endured the fear and apprehension that gnawed at his heart as he gave them the orders.

“Understood. Captain, you should go early and return early!” Several guards were so happy when they heard that the Captain would be forfeiting his share of the profits when he was going to handle an issue with the Country Lord.

“The Griffin is ready!” The smart Greyhound even quickly held the flying messenger’s mount for the captain and waited for the Captain to go off.

“Goodbye, brothers,” The Captain of the guard turned the Griffin around and said this intentionally.

Then, he frantically urged the Griffin and made it go as fast as possible.

Two days later.

The Griffin, who was overworked by flying at high speeds both day and night, fell to the ground unsupported and had already been abandoned by the Captain of the guard.

The Captain of the guard used all his strength to rush his journey desperately. What he desired the most was not to report to the Country Lord— news like this probably already reached the Country Lord’s side long ago, so there was no need to do something pointless. What he had to do was to save his own life, and that was by desperately fleeing to the Capital where the Country Lord was stationed as that would be the safest place.

The Green Willow City, the capital of the Zhi Feng Country.

He already caught the faintest glimpse of the tall spire that was located in the capital.

Green Willow City, a peaceful place where no war ever broke out.

Due to the powerful strength of the Country Lord of Zhi Feng, it was also located in the rear of the war zone of the Heavenly Splendour Territory. Here, thousands of miles of fertile soil were prosperous. No army nor mob could ever launch their attack here… The Captain of the guard only sighed and relaxed slightly when he faintly saw the Green Willow City becoming vividly clearer.

It’s done— he was safe now that he was able to get to this point.

He took off theCaptain’s clothes and threw them all away. Then, he put on some deerskin he obtained from hunting and disguised as a hunter who had lived in the forest area for a long time. Although he didn’t know how the war progressed, he had already abandoned his previous identity as the Captain of the city gate’s guard and decided to change his identity. He would infiltrate Green Willow City and live anew in this peaceful city. Having a certain amount of strength also meant that he would not need to worry about going hungry. As long as his place of residency was safe enough, he wasn’t going to worry about ranks or positions at all.

After checking himself high and low, he couldn’t find a flaw with his disguise at all.

The former Captain of the guard just flew out of the forest area and landed at the city gates of Green Willow City. He handed the guard the identity certificate and tax that he had prepared earlier, accompanied by a smile as he said that he needed to enter the city to buy some furs.

“Poor bastard!” The guard snorted with disgust as he forcibly extorted two more gold coins out of his pockets before he waved him towards the gates, “Scram!”

“Wow, look! Look over there! There is a luxurious slave-catching team coming ove. Oh my God, this slave-catching team seems to have obtained a lot of profits! The back of the big-footed dragon is actually carrying a golden throne; which slave-catching team is this?” Several guards discussed the sight in surprise.

Hearing this, the former Captain of the guards of Hei Ling City stiffened.

He looked back in disbelief.

In desolution, he found that the slave-hunting team that he had seen in Hei Ling City before was slowly approaching Green Willow City.

Their team seems to have grown a bit bigger; there were more than a dozen mercenaries in charge of being escorts and there were also a few more prison carts. The only thing that remains unchanged was the Dromaeosaur-riding pathfinder thief. His eyes were still bright and his face was still smiling cheekily… And on the base of the golden throne was none other than the same slave girl and the girl who carried a baby in her arms. On the peak of the golden throne sat the two identically-dressed silver-armoured warriors as well.

The former Captain of the guard was so frightened that his face turned pale and he nearly collapsed on the ground.

He knew the distance between Hei Ling City and Green Willow City.

Even the flying Griffin took about ten days to get here, let alone relying on a mere big-footed earth dragon!
