“Ignorant being, you’re asking for death!” Sky Execution, who had been staying by Yue Yang’s side this entire time, raised his hand of lightning. With one blow, the two assassins were about to be sent flying.

“Ah?” Seeing Sky Execution’s attack, the three Country Lords couldn’t help but feel goosebumps form on their skin. Even though it was predicted, they still underestimated the power of this fella!

A gorgeous starlight flashed.

Hu Lei discovered that these starlights actually came from behind him.

Due to some misunderstandings with the Young Master Titan previously, although he was forgiven, Hu Lei always wanted to apologise better.

However, with his status, he could only sit at the outermost periphery of the main hall, seated with a group of Heaven Rankers who came to celebrate. After he discovered the transparent thief, the thief was already captured, so when he found the second wave of sneak attackers, he had already increased his strength and was ready to help whenever he was needed. Perhaps the Young Master Titan was not really in danger, but Hu Lei didn’t mind showing off a bit.

At this time, from behind him, thousands of dazzling starlight burst forth, making Hu Lei stunned.

Was there an enemy in the back?

It was too late to turn around.

Those incomparably beautiful starlights have already radiated into thousands of stars and turned into a dreamlike galaxy. Everyone in the audience was deeply fascinated by this perfect and unparalleled galaxy.

Hu Lei found that everyone in the audience was dumbfounded.

Only Country Lord Zhi Feng was moving slowly, but Hu Lei was horrified to find out— how could Country Lord Zhi Feng be crawling like an ant? Wasn’t this speed too slow?

Country Lord Zhi Feng knew what was going on. It was none other than the power of the Domain, or the special ability of some kind of treasure that was at least at a Quasi-Divine level. This terrifying force restricted the movements of everyone in the audience for a short period of time. The Country Lord Zhi Feng couldn’t take care of the Divine Blood anymore as now, the most important thing was to protect the Young Master Titan. What the enemy wanted to seize was not only the Divine Blood, but also the life of this Young Master Titan. Country Lord Zhi Feng couldn’t even care about his favourite Zhu Guang as he rushed to the motionless Young Master Titan first.

As Country Lord Zhi Feng was about to intercept the assassin just now, when he returned again, he was already at a certain distance from the Young Master Titan.

Although the distance was less than two metres, the distance was as far away as a moat, making the Country Lord Zhi Feng feel that it was insurmountable at this speed.

On the other side, with a single strike of the lightning hand, Sky Execution, which blasted away the two powerful enemies, was also returning quickly. However, his speed was incomparably different from that of Country Lord Zhi Feng. He was just a little faster but he was also far from returning to Yue Yang’s side.

“…” Zhu Guang’s lips trembled slightly as if she wanted to say something, but in the end she didn’t say anything.

She returned to her motionless appearance and waited for the situation to change.

The two powerful assassins who were shot by Sky Execution seemed to have some sort of treasures to protect them as they ignored the stagnation of this galaxy.

They were as fast as the released arrows from a sharp bow as they launched attacks at Yue Yang furiously. A strange light flashed under their hands as the two purple-black assassination daggers shone with the light of death.

“No!”

Country Lord Zhi Feng was so anxious that he wanted to cry tearlessly. They could kill anyone, but why did he have to kill this Young Master from the Heaven Realm? This Young Master has a lot of treasures so it was no problem to rob him, but they must not kill him. Once he was killed, would this Heavenly Splendour Territory still exist? However, Country Lord Zhi Feng was powerless to stop it, so he could only watch this tragedy unfold.

Under the watchful eyes of the public, just as the two assassins were about to stab the Young Master Titan’s heart with their daggers, suddenly, the pet dog that had been crawling under the table and gnawing at bones jumped up.

It moved like a rabbit with lightning fast agility.

Even the power of the Galaxy of Stars, which was estimated to be at least a Quasi-Divine artifact, could not stop its speedy activities. That was because the Country Lord Zhi Feng, Qinglang, Wanjiao, Louluo and the other Heaven Rankers found that this stupid-looking dog turned out to be a Divine Beast!

The will of the Divine Beast exploded wildly, offsetting the instantaneous suppression of the treasure’s power.

It summoned the Grimoire and first blocked the fatal blow of the two assassins with a shield before it loaded its immobile master into the Grimoire World.

They turned into a swirling black fire smoke that spun like a tornado and escaped from the hall of the Green Willow Palace.

The two assassins who missed their first shot were about to pursue, but the other monster who had not reacted to the suppression had stopped them. They were well-informed so they couldn’t help but exclaim, “An Advanced Wisdom Earth Divine Soldier?”

To a certain extent, although the Earth Divine Soldier was rare, it was not the most unique thing.

However, it was an absolutely rare existence for an Earth Divine Soldier that knew how to transform into weapons and have the wisdom to cover its master’s escape at a critical juncture. In other words, between the ordinary Earth Divine Soldiers and those with Advanced Wisdom, it was undoubtedly comparable to the gap between Heaven Ranked Beasts and Divine Beasts. Although one was strong, it was not destined to have a very bright future; but the other was not only powerful, but also destined to have an immeasurable future. The same was true here: one was a normal Earth Divine Soldier, but the other had immeasurable potential to grow and achieve greatness!

The Tao Tie Blade was blindingly sharp as its whole body intercepted the enemy like an iron wall.

The two assassins exhausted all means but could not pass its interception range. They could only watch the Divine Beast leave, sighing helplessly.

Fortunately, this Earth Divine Soldier was eager to protect its master. When its master was completely out of danger, he immediately turned into a black light. Just like the birth of an artifact, it directly split the space and returned to its master directly through the ability of anti-summoning.

Country Lord Zhi Feng was moved to death.

That inconspicuous black dog turned out to be a Divine Beast and that ferocious-looking monster, the Earth Divine Soldier, had such a high level of intelligence— what a true show-stopper! No matter who the enemy was and who offended the Young Master Titan, their fate was destined to be difficult. Especially now that Master Titan had escaped danger safely, the enemy’s wishful thinking had failed and it was time for their turn to be cursed with fatal injuries.

Qinglang and other two Country Lords were scared to death.

How could they ever have guessed that the dog that has been chewing on the bones turned out to be a Divine Beast?

Fortunately, they didn’t take the action to rob the Young Master Titan; otherwise, they really didn’t know how they were going to meet their end. With the Divine Beast and the Earth Divine Soldier by his side, paired with Sky Execution, the Dragon Emperor and the Underworld Emperor, not to mention the numerous Heaven Rankers, it was impossible for anyone to touch the Young Master Titan. No joke, they might as well have a better shot at attacking the Sacred Light Domain Emperor!

The Young Master Titan finally escaped.

However, the crisis was still there.

While the power of treasure suppression was still in effect, the two assassins did not escape as one of them rushed towards the Country Lord Zhi Feng.

The other one made his way to Zhu Guang.

Faster than the two assassins, someone in the hall floated in from the door of the Hall of Glory as leisurely as returning to his own room. His movements were slow and steady, but his speed was better than the lightning-fast assassins. Everyone could clearly see that a golden man had walked into the hall. His appearance was so handsome that made other people sigh. Besides the charismatic Young Master Titan, even Sky Execution had flattered a bit due to his appearance. This handsome man wearing a golden dragon-patterned battle armour was holding a blazing lightsaber in his right hand. Even if he never wielded it, the sword’s aura seemed to have penetrated everyone’s heart.

In his left hand, he held a Star Galaxy Necklace that shone as bright as the Milky Way.

Under the watchful eyes of the public, the man returned the sword to its sheath. He held the golden box that was in Chief Steward Ye’s hand and then shattered the space with some kind of golden light power before disappearing instantly. The fists of Sky Execution and Country Lord Zhi Feng slammed at where he last stopped, but they hit nothing.

“Holding the Quasi-Divine artifact, the Light Divine Sword, and the Star Galaxy Necklace— this person is the Vengeance Domain Emperor, it must be,” Qinglang shouted.

“Humph!” Country Lord Zhi Feng didn’t think so. This man was indeed very similar to the Vengeance Domain Emperor, but if he was going to come, there was no need to reveal his true face. With the power of the Domain Emperor, he could completely kill all the people in the audience before leaving. For a drop of Divine Blood, why would he use such frivolous means? With the ability of the Vengeance Domain Emperor, it would be far easier to make friends with Young Master Titan than himself, so why deliberately choose murder? Someone must have deliberately pretended to be the Vengeance Domain Emperor. Especially when the Young Master Titan had just escaped safely, he took his time to reveal himself. He probably did so with the intention to frame someone else and put the blame away from him.

In the Heavenly Splendour Territory, how many people would have the strength to push the blame towards the Vengeance Domain Emperor?

Besides the other two Domain Emperors, there’s only the Central Palace left. Would the two Domain Emperors have the guts to assassinate the Young Master Titan of the Upper Heaven Realm in public like this? No matter how stupid the Sacred Light Domain Emperor and the Rage Domain Emperor were, they probably wouldn’t do such a thing, right? If that was the case, then besides the Heaven Rankers of the Upper Realm that played a part in the roles of the Four Major Families, there would be…

The Country Lord Zhi Feng was not an idiot. He thought about it and was enraged, however, he just didn’t want to reveal the person in front of a crowd.

“Help!” Zhu Guang exclaimed as she lost her composure.

Without the role of the treasure’s special ability, Star Galaxy, Country Lord Zhi Feng could easily evade the enemy’s attack. However, Zhu Guang couldn’t.

The weak woman was almost killed in one blow by the assassin.

Fortunately, the loyal Captain of the Guard, Yu Chao, sacrificed his life to save her as he barely managed to block the assassin’s attack.

The Country Lord Zhi Feng didn’t have the time to explain the accident to Sky Execution and others, so he rushed to rescue his most beloved woman. Sky Execution also went to the captured transparent thief, ready to interrogate whoever gave the order to assassinate the Young Master Titan and make an enemy out of a noble clan of the Upper Heaven Realm.

The two assassins were at the end of the road, fighting to their deaths with every move.

Country Lord Zhi Feng was determined to take them down and torture them by force. As he dealt with them calmly, he gestured to Zhu Guang to stay away. It was best for her to retreat to Sky Execution and other strong men’s side to seek protection.

“After I die, you, and everyone present, will be cursed. If you want to survive, you must take the Liberty Queen to the Central Island of Zhi Feng. Hahaha, you are all caught up in the trap,” The transparent thief laughed madly as his originally transparent body gradually emerged with countless rune patterns, rapidly expanding the mutation. Finally, he exploded silently, splashing the fragments of the mutated black rune pattern on the warriors in the entire hall.

“…” Sky Execution fought with his energy but found that it was of no use at all. The rune fragments also disappeared into his arms and disappeared into his body and he couldn’t help but frown.

“There is indeed an abnormal energy invasion from these fragments— this is a curse, it’s a curse!” Country Lord Wanjiao yelled in disgust.

“Damn, let’s take down those two damn assassins together. Brother Zhi Feng, let’s help you!” Just when Country Lord Qinglang was willing to step forward and help the Country Lord Zhi Feng, suddenly the two assassins emerged signs of pre-explosions.

“My Lord, go!” The loyal Yu Chao rushed to the enemy. He was willing to die with the enemy if it meant that his Lord would remain alive.

“You want to explode? Hmph! It’s not that easy!” COuntry Lord Zhi Feng knew that it was not impossible for them to explode, but it took time. In the hands of powerful people such as himself and Sky Execution, could it really be easy for them to explode? Even if two couldn’t be stopped, at least one could be captured, right? Otherwise, do these desperados really think that his title as a Country Lord was just for show?

Country Lord Zhi Feng rushed to attack the two assassins as he stood with Yu Chao.

Country Lords Qinglang, Wanjiao, and Louluo have already rushed over. Sky Execution, the Dragon Emperor and the Underworld Emperor also surrounded them in circles. If the two wanted to self-destruct, they had to go through them first!

Just when the situation became a foregone conclusion, misfortune broke out.

Yu Chao, who had always been loyal, suddenly stabbed a dagger into the back of Country Lord Zhi Feng’s heart. This poisonous assassination dagger was exactly the same as those in the hands of the two assassins. Ignoring the Body-protecting Dragon Leather Armour of the Country Lord Zhi Feng, it directly penetrated his back muscles and impaled itself into his heart. The steward took Zhu Guang in one hand as a flash of golden brilliance lit up the hall.

Yu Chao, the two assassins, the steward and Zhu Guang all broke open the space weirdly and teleported away in the moment before anyone could react.

Their way of leaving was exactly the same as the way the handsome man who stole the Divine Blood left.

The Country Lord Zhi Feng fell to the ground and blood was gushing from where the dagger was impaled.

No one spoke for a long time.

Nobody could have imagined that things would turn out like this, and even the three Country Lords Qinglang, Wanjiao, and Louluo, who were going to come to make a mess, could never have imagined that this would happen.

chapters.title: Chapter 949
chapters.body:
Chapter 949

Chapter 949: Liberty Queen?

Sky Execution and the Dragon Emperor looked at each other as they remained silent.

However, Chief Steward Ye, who had reacted in shock, stood up tremblingly, “My Lord, if you have any last words, please entrust it to sir Sky Execution. I have a humble status, so I am afraid it would be difficult for me to take on a heavy responsibility.”

Although flustered, Chief Steward Ye was not a fool.

In these critical moments, his first choice would be the mighty Sky Execution.

Now that Young Master Titan was not present, the three Country Lords Qinglang, Wanjiao and Louluo were staring at each other. If they were allowed to intervene, not only the Green Willow City, but perhaps the entire Zhi Feng Country would be split among the three. Therefore, it would be better to entrust the Lord’s will to Sky Execution and wait for Young Master Titan to return. From there, he would arrange the matters, hopefully fulfilling any last wishes of the Country Lord Zhi Feng.

“It appears that you are still the loyal one…” Country Lord Zhi Feng smiled bitterly and suddenly stood up.

The energy on his body burst out like a blazing light, and he, who was originally weak and dying, quickly recovered to what his state was before. If it weren’t for the fact that a poisonous assassination dagger was still stuck in the back of the heart and the blood dripping from his body, people would doubt whether their vision was misleading.

Chief Steward Ye, the other City Lords and the Heaven Ranked warriors couldn’t help cheering loudly.

In any case, they didn’t want Country Lord Zhi Feng to die.

If Country Lord Zhi Feng were to die, then they would have to be under a different dictator.

After the change of ownership, their current prosperity and wealth would not necessarily remain the same. It would be okay if they were entrusted to Young Master Titan or Sky Execution; they didn’t seem to be the type who would take advantage of the situation and control the Green Willow City according to their selfish needs. It was very likely that everyone would have their own share of dividends and rights. However, if they were to fall under the ownership of the three Country Lords, the hopes in their hearts would flicker and dim. The influential City Lords would also probably be eliminated and the people would have to rebuild from scratch. No one was willing to see things regress this way, so seeing Country Lord Zhi Feng standing up and reverting to his optimal form made everyone cheer in joy.

Qinglang, Wanjiao and Louluo were slightly disappointed, but their gazes were sharp.

They noticed something that ordinary Heaven Rankers weren’t able to pick out and their lips curved into a small smile.

Though Country Lord Zhi Feng recovered, did he really heal back to his state as before? That would be impossible as there was a dagger in the heart and the blade was draped with poison. Country Lord Zhi Feng, who was not an elemental body, would have no chance of being immune to the poison. The assassination was actually borderline successful.

The current Country Lord Zhi Feng was just showing his strength on the surface.

However, with Sky Execution and the others present, Qinglang and the other two Country Lords remained unmoving, pretending that they didn’t see through his facade.

“Sir Sky Execution, Zhi Feng has a lot to be sorry for, but I don’t hope to amend this merely by verbal apologies. Today’s incident was entirely the fault of Zhi Feng alone and it has implicated Young Master Titan and all the other distinguished guests. Everything else can be put aside as long as the Young Master Titan is fine, but can I know how we are to locate him to make sure that he is safe?” After saying this, the Country Lord Zhi Feng fell silent again. Young Master Titan was also nearly assassinated just now, but nothing should happen to him as that would lead to the downfall of the entire Green Willow City.

“There is Hui Tai Lang guarding him, so there shouldn’t be a problem,” The Dragon Emperor temporarily stayed in the hall as Sky Execution quickly left.

Ten minutes later.

Sky Execution brought back Hui Tai Lang, who was not astonishing in appearance but was actually a Divine Beast that shocked everyone.

It summoned the Grimoire, glittering with golden light as it reflected its luminance in everyone’s eyes.

Seeing the Divine Dog Beast summoning a Grimoire of a Platinum Level, the audience, including Country Lord Zhi Feng, were extremely ashamed and embarrassed. That was because amongst the numerous Heaven Rankers present, only Zhi Feng and the other three Country Lords were able to summon their Grimoires. Country Lord Zhi Feng owned a Silver Level Grimoire, which was the strongest grade out of the four of them, but it wasn’t even close to Gold, let alone Platinum.

The talents in the Heavenly Splendour Territory have severely diminished to the point where there were only a select few who could contract a Grimoire.

Moreover, some Country Lords would kill the younger generations that could summon a Grimoire themselves just to protect the ‘rarity’ of the title and prevent them from attempting to take over even though they might not even have the intention to do so in the first place. This, in turn, caused the Grimoire summoners to become even rarer, to the point where it was the norm to only be owned by the Country Lords.

“Oh great Divine Beast, I was too negligent, please forgive me,” Chief Steward Ye quickly led the first servants of Leader Jin’s class to bow to Hui Tai Lang and pay their homage to him.

“Aooooo!” Hui Tai Lang proudly made an ‘okay’ gesture that he learned from Yue Yang before releasing his master from the Grimoire World.

Everyone watched nervously, hoping that the Young Master Titan who was nearly assassinated, would not be angered by the situation.

They guessed that a sheltered Young Master like him would probably have never felt such intensity before. If it weren’t for the protection of Divine Beasts, even if he didn’t die, everyone thought that the Young Master Titan would at the very least suffer chronic injuries.

As soon as student Yue Yang came out, he immediately burst into laughter, “Not bad, that was very exciting. I didn’t expect that there would be assassins here at the birthday banquet, but that was really fun. By the way, have those assassins been caught? I’m going to interrogate them myself. Prepare a whip and a soldering iron; I want them to know what the torture is like!”

After listening to the words of this Young Master Titan, everyone felt relieved and laughed.

As long as he was alright, everything else could come later!

But having assassins in a birthday banquet was definitely not an enjoyable thing. Something like this shouldn’t have occurred at all, so they hoped that this was going to be the first and the last time an incident as such would happen. Otherwise, it was going to be hard to settle it in the future!

Country Lord Zhi Feng was also relieved. Having an attack against him was something he couldn’t care less about, but it would be bad if the Young Master was injured in any sort of way, that would have been absolutely terrible. He stepped forward to give a slight salute and apologised before he sighed, “The enemies came in with a plan, one that brother Zhi Feng was unable to stop. Fortunately, brother Titan hadn’t sustained any sort of injury, otherwise things would have been bad. But now, the assassins have disappeared into thin air, and they even took my beloved concubine with them…”

He recounted the cause and the effects of the incident.

Of course, the details about his suspicions that it was the act of the Central Palace that came to assassinate the Young Master Titan and trying to pin the blame on the Vengeance Domain Emperor— that should be left unsaid for the time being.

It will be brought up, but not in front of Qinglang and the others.

He was going to wait for a good opportunity to tell the Young Master Titan in secret.

I haven’t lost yet.

With Young Master Titan standing on his side and with the support of the other powerful rankers such as Sky Execution, the Dragon Emperor and the Underworld Emperor, he still had the power to fight!

“It’s fine that the Divine Blood was taken away, I can give you another drop, so don’t fret about it. As for the beauty Zhu Guang, Titan feels sorry that she was dragged into this unfortunate mess. If I get any clues of her whereabouts and can somehow find her on the Central Island of the Zhi Feng, I will definitely do my best to rescue her and bring her back. These lumps of assassins were too arrogant; how dare they ruin this Young Master’s fun! I have decided that I’m going to see to the end of this in person, otherwise, they will think that this Young Master Titan is an easy target to bully! Zhong Guan, get my guards and assemble together; this Young Master is going to beat up those useless idiots into shreds!” Student Yue Yang waved his hands exaggeratedly, indicating that the battle was not over as the battlefield was merely moved to the Central Island of Zhi Feng!

Even for someone who was as stone cold as Country Lord Zhi Feng, he was moved by the declaration. Young Master Titan was his lifesaver— with such a friend like him, there was nothing else he could ask for!

Qinglang and the others were so jealous.

What the heck? What sort of undeservingly good luck does that mutt Zhi Feng have? He didn’t even blame Zhi Feng for losing the Divine Blood, but he even offered to give him another drop? What?

If it weren’t for the fact that the bastard was injured and unable to absorb the Divine Blood, perhaps the Young Master Titan might have even sent a new batch of the Divine Blood straight away to this hall to comfort him. Freaking hell, the luck of Zhi Feng, that idiot, is way too good to be true. Why didn’t Yu Chao just stab him to death just now?

Qinglang, Wanjiao and Louluo exchanged glances and nodded secretly.

They have seen the weakness of Zhi Feng; there was no doubt that it was his ‘heart’.

For the traitor Yu Chao, someone who knew Country Lord Zhi Feng the best, there was no reason for him not to strike the weakest points of his enemy.

For an ordinary Heaven Ranker, the heart was not a weakness, so for a high grade Country Lord, it wouldn’t even matter if his heart was destroyed. For example, Qinglang himself could fight completely without hindrance even if his heart was to be shattered. However, Zhi Feng, who was also at the level of the Country Lord, indisputably fell to the ground when a dagger pierced his heart. If Sky Execution was not present, Zhi Feng would definitely have died if he was attacked again.

Which meant that if they really wanted to kill the bastard Zhi Feng, they had to aim for his heart once more.

The stubborn man was just trying to keep himself from worsening his injury and putting on a straight face.

Country Lord Zhi Feng gave Qinglang and the others a cold look and spoke of words in a secret language towards Yue Yang’s ear. Apart from Yue Yang, no one else could hear him. Yue Yang nodded again and again while Zhi Feng’s eyes became more and more gloomy; instead, Qinglang and the others felt chills run down their spine.

Could it be that Country Lord Zhi Feng was going to put the blame on them? Or did he figure out something suspicious that he could relate to their actions?

No one knew the truth.

But the young Young Master Titan, who was surrounded by the crowd, quickly reacted. He clenched his fists and smiled, showing a murderous glint in his eyes that made the spectators shudder, “Hmph, how interesting. Since that’s the case, then we have to prepare seriously for the battle. It’s been a long time since we’ve been this pumped up! There are many people who want to move this Titan, but how many of them will have the last laugh?”

The people present were surprised to find that when the Young Master decided to become serious, they could collectively feel a sense of coercion pressing down on them.

His strength was not high as he was probably something close to a Quasi-Heaven Rank and he relied on his treasures to boost himself to a power comparable to the Heaven Rankers. However, even the strongest rankers like Country Lord Qinglang and the others could feel the coercion emanating from Yue Yang, let alone the other ordinary Heaven Rankers.

‘In the Upper Realm, no one is weak’— whoever mentioned that, it was definitely true!

They then waited for Chief Steward Ye and the others to prepare the luxury airship, setting course to the Central Island of Zhi Feng.

Ming Ri Hao, the guy who hid himself to indulge in his meals, took advantage of the time where no one was paying attention and fisted Yue Yang, “Damn, your plan really worked. The thing that I respect the most is the fact that those assassins were really cooperative. What sort of methods did you use to get to this stage?”

“I used my brain,” Student Yue Yang answered him vaguely, conspicuously despising this lazy guy for not doing enough work.

“If that side’s forces appear, do you have enough people?” Ming Ri Hao was used to getting scrutinising gazes from Yue Yang and was no longer angered by that.

“What do you mean by ‘you’? Remember, it’s ‘we’. If you are lazy again, this Young Master will leave without saying a word and give you the entire Divine Grimoire. If you have the ability, you can slowly kill the brats in the Central Palace who like to play sneak attacks from behind. It’s easy— deal with them and the Divine Grimoire is all yours,” Yue Yang warned Ming Ri Hao not to pretend to be dead, or else their cooperation would collapse in one shot.

This was what Ming Ri Hao was most afraid of, so he quickly waved his hand, “Hey, hey, I’ll say, Third Young Master, let’s just talk in a civil manner, okay? Don’t get angry. I wasn’t being lazy before; I just acted according to your plan. Besides, if Zhu Guang didn’t go away, she would have seen through my disguise the moment I appeared— wouldn’t that be a bad thing? Whatever happens next, I will follow your orders. If you ask me to go to the west, I won’t diverge to the east; if you want me to shut up, I ensure that there would be no noise from me!”

“Is there really a Divine Grimoire here?” The Dragon Emperor was still a little unconvinced.

“I actually hope the Heavenly Splendour Territory doesn’t have it,” Yue Yang felt that it was best if there were no Divine Grimoires here. Without it, he wouldn’t need to go against someone psychotic like Ji Wu Ri.

“I’m not interested in the Divine Grimoire, but I must test the Palace Master myself,” These were the words of the battling madman Sky Execution. He knew that it was impossible for him to contract the Divine Grimoire so he would rather let the pervert Yue Yang figure it out. However, he wanted to try out the skills of the Palace Master.

“There will be a chance for you to do that, but if you are not afraid of hearing the truth from me, I’m going to say something you might not like— all of you combined together will probably not be enough to fight one of them,” Ming Ri Hao knew that the strengths of the Palace Masters.

“It’s useless to try and intimidate them for they have already killed two Deputy Palace Masters and are darned proud of themselves. If you don’t let them hit the nail, they’re not going to believe it,” Yue Yang snickered happily.

“You guys killed two Deputy Palace Masters?” Ming Ri Hao was so surprised that his eyes were about to fall out.

When did the standards of the Deputy Palace Masters become something so common like cabbage?

No wonder Sky Execution said that he wanted to challenge the Palace Master!

Feng Ji and Jin Yun, the two newcomers who have just joined, were going to burst into tears. They knew where they stood in the ranks of power. Fighting with the Central Palace? Their future was bleak!

Just as Yue Yang, Sky Execution, Ming Ri Hao and others were discussing how to plan the next step, suddenly, a sound rang out from a far-away direction. Like a clear spring of the soul, it instantly sent the people a wave of serenity. The hearts of the people have been cleansed again, calming the turbid tremors of everyone’s hearts.

The Bull Brothers who were eating and drinking immediately jumped up, “Liberty Queen!”

chapters.title: Chapter 950
chapters.body:
Chapter 950

Chapter 950: Inspiration and Enlightenment

Yue Yang had seen many beauties.

Some were gentle like water, some were charming and cute; some were hot and sexy whereas some were charming and affectionate; there were also some who were deadly and enchanting, and some acted as cool as a snow queen.

There were almost no identical beauties in the world as every woman had her own unique personality.

[T/L Note: Facts right there!]

Of course, apart from their individualities, each beauty would have many similarities.

For example, their beautiful appearances.

However, Yue Yang had set his sights on such a different beauty like the Liberty Queen.

The woman who was detained in the prison cart by the Heaven Rankers was not the most beautiful woman Yue Yang had ever seen. On the contrary, her appearance was not as good as Yue Yang imagined, but this Queen was definitely the most divine, tolerant and kind-hearted woman that Yue Yang had ever seen. Although she was the Liberty Queen, she did not have the appearance like any other queen in the world. She resembled the likes of an ordinarily poor girl as she walked in tattered clothes. Even her tattered fabrics that could barely cover her frame were not spotlessly clean as they were more or less stained by spots of dust and dried blood.

The way she was dressed was practically equivalent to those starving citizens flocking on the roads of the streets, waiting for their downfall.

However, none of this hindered her beauty.

It also didn’t do anything to hinder Yue Yang’s surprisement towards her!

Even as she was in the prison cart, she smiled as if she wasn’t being taken to prison but instead, into a castle that vowed to protect her.

That sort of smile was not a forced smile, it was an arc formed by the pure kindness and benevolence of one’s soul.

That kind of smile could exude a type of maternity’s unique gentleness, like the bright moon in the sky.

That kind of smile that could only be completely exuded from the heart. Like a clear spring in the forest, it was capable of washing away the anger in the hearts of people at a glance. When she nodded slightly to the hungry people who were kneeling on both sides of the street, she reassured them with a smile. Yue Yang suddenly felt that this was a powerful force as it was absolutely impossible for it to be possessed by an ordinary Heaven Ranker. It had to be a powerhouse above the level of an Innate Supreme who possessed an immovable will to hold onto such a powerful force of belief.

“Don’t cry; be it hunger or pain, I will definitely help to make it go away. No matter the situation, don’t give up. Hold onto hope, because there will always be hope!”

“I’m fine, you don’t have to worry.”

“Live well!”

The words of the Liberty Queen were not very passionate and there was no frenzied agitation either.

It was just her very bland way of talking, as if telling a fact or giving advice to her relatives at home. However, even Yue Yang, a powerful being who had reached the realm of Innate Supremacy and had an unshakable sense of belief in himself, did not feel that she was wrong after listening to her words. It was as if she seemed to have no psychological resistance to this ‘ridiculous persistence and belief’.

If it was anyone else, Yue Yang would definitely sneer when he heard such comforting words spewed out of their mouths.

By doing nothing, will there really be hope?

Lying around waiting for their impending deaths would bring them hope?

However, when the Liberty Queen said this, Yue Yang surprisingly found that he had no intention of refuting it at all.

The reason was because what the Liberty Queen said seemed to be right… What was it that could make his immovable will not resist the irony of the statement? Her words wielded the power that could rival the will of God! Actually, it might even be beyond the general will of the Gods as even Yue Yang could not resist but acknowledge her words.

Yue Yang discovered unexpectedly that although the Liberty Queen’s eyes were bright, they had no visual focus.

Which meant that she was actually a blind girl!

The Liberty Queen, who wasn’t able to see anything in the world, used a form of insight to watch the world.

When she smiled, there was a unique kind of tenderness and tolerance which emerged in her bright eyes that bore no focus at all. She also had an indescribable tenderness to her face which directly touched the hearts of others, even affecting Yue Yang’s own heart that he usually closed off so often.

In front of this Liberty Queen, even a Heaven Ranked powerhouse would be humbled.

It wasn’t because she was strong, but it was due to her tenderness and kindness that sympathised with all beings.

A woman like her was destined to be respected by the world even if she was locked in a prison cart, even if she wore ragged clothes and even if she had no deterrent power.

The Liberty Queen’s hands were two flawless and fair porcelain arms.. However, her legs no longer existed— in other words, the parts below her lower abdomen have disappeared. Her lower body, Yue Yang had heard before, had been sacrificed in order to eliminate the diseases and curses of the devout and hungry people.

Even her body was not completely flesh and blood.

She was basically a half-flesh and half-energy entity at this point. If it weren’t for an unknown Divine power that kept her alive, the Liberty Queen would have died long ago. And this kind of Divine power was the same kind that Yue Yang extracted from the Bull Brothers. In other words, the Bull Brothers’ lives were actually saved by the Liberty Queen, not by a certain god.

How could a free queen who was not even a Heaven Ranker use Divine power?

Yue Yang felt very confused.

“Really? Have many people been cursed? I understand,” The Liberty Queen nodded after listening to this. Without her visual focus, she even ‘looked around’ at the people around her, a special habit she still retained. Yue Yang could immediately judge that this Liberty Queen’s blindness was not by birth, but for some reason, perhaps in order to save the lives of some people, the eyesight of hers was sacrificed forever. The Liberty Queen made a condition, “As long as the residents of Green Willow City can have a full meal, then I am willing to lift this curse for everyone. However, I don’t understand why it must be moved to the Central Island of Zhi Feng… Maybe, this curse is a revenge curse that needed a type of special resolution.”

“Anyway, please come with us to Central Island. We are all fine, but there were some very important guests from the Upper Realm who came to congratulate us and were cursed by mistake. But all this is really the fault of Zhi Feng,” Country Lord Zhi Feng introduced Yue Yang, the Young Master Titan, as well as Sky Execution and others to the Liberty Queen.

Finally, the Country Lord of Zhi Feng guaranteed, “Whether the curse is successfully resolved or not, the hungry people in Green Willow City will receive their fair share of sanctuary.”

The Liberty Queen listened to him and a smile bloomed on her unblemished face.

A feeling of joy bloomed from the bottom of her heart.

She bowed deeply with both hands, “Goodness will always be rewarded; Country Lord Zhi Feng will definitely get the best return for today’s good deeds.”

Yue Yang was silent. He had seen countless beauties in his life. Not just his relatives, but there were also a bunch of women that he met who bore different and drastic personalities. Yue Yang had seen people like the Scarlet Concubine who was obsessed with her love towards the Scarlet Emperor, swearing to follow him all the way without a sense of self-appreciation; he also seen women like the beauty Zhu Guang who was doted on by thousands of people yet still possessed selfish thoughts. He had seen those like Fairy Qi Luo who fought with the enemy and finally perished together, remaining trapped in the same world; he had also even seen those like the Navagraha Empress who pursued extreme strength to the extent where she bet her fate on her entire clan… But he had never seen someone as selfless and kind like the Liberty Queen.

In the eyes of Yue Yang, the Liberty Queen, who was indescribable in words, was the biggest idiot!

But when he saw her with his own eyes, he couldn’t help but be moved by her!

What kind of power is it exactly that could make him feel this way?

She knew that the Country Lord Zhi Feng, Qinglang and the others were completely evil. They were prepared to burn her alive and never once sympathised with her as the binds of the ropes cut into her skin when she was confined in the prison cart. So why did she blindly agree to their conditions to resolve the curse with just one request from them?

If this Liberty Queen was his own girl, Yue Yang would definitely take off her pants and spank her.

This was too much of an idiotic behaviour to let go!

However, seeing the broken body and the gently kind smile of the Liberty Queen, Yue Yang couldn’t call her an idiot.

In this world, there would always be some people who loved selflessly without a price at all. Without asking for any reward at all, they were the type of person who was already suffering and exhausted, yet were willing to silently endure and fully sacrifice themselves… For example, his own Si-niang.

Si-niang was not like the Liberty Queen, who treated everyone kindly and selflessly. Si-niang’s empathy merely extended within the scopes of her family members.

But in some ways, she was the same as the Liberty Queen.

Her dedication towards Yue Yang, Yue Bing and the little girl was more than that of the Liberty Queen’s to the world. It could be said that Si-niang concentrated her kind of great love on Yue Yang and his siblings, which shaped him into the happy person he is when he spent time with his family. He couldn’t be a selfless saint himself, but Yue Yang didn’t want to deny this kind of contribution, because if he were to deny it, it would mean denying Si-niang.

When the Liberty Queen boarded the luxury airship and decided to accompany the Country Lord of Zhi Feng to meet the enemy on the Central Island of the Wind-stopping Swamp, the hungry people of Green Willow City finally got free food for the first time.

They all knew.

These were the blessings that the Liberty Queen made in exchange for their lives as they couldn’t do anything else but cry and pray.

Yue Yang saw everything in his eyes, and took the biggest doubt in his heart into the airship. When the Country Lord Zhi Feng and others discussed with Sky Execution on how to prevent the enemy from setting up an ambush and siege, Yue Yang approached the Liberty Queen and asked, “If the Country Lord Zhi Feng doesn’t keep his promise, what would you do? He has power so there is no need to keep his promise, and even if he breaks his promise, you have no power to stop him.”

The Liberty Queen ‘looked’ at Yue Yang with unfocused eyes, showing a smile like a spring breeze, “Are you the Young Master Titan from the Upper Realm? Although you appear to be very rebellious, I can see that you are the only one who has a golden soul among many people… Here, except you, no one else would ask the question you did just now. That is to say, except you, not many people have empathy, especially when it comes to warriors with great power. They won’t remember the meaning of empathy until they lose their power— in fact, empathy is the greatest power! Those who have empathy are the most powerful people!”

“How is it possible? I don’t agree with your statement!” Yue Yang never felt that someone with empathy was the most powerful person, nor did he feel that empathy was the greatest strength of all.

“Think about it, if you had the ultimate power and looked back at the world, wouldn’t you all be as pitiful as ants? Watching them busy themselves with their vulgar and miserable lives, getting through emotions for the slightest benefit, for a momentary fame. Even if they try to fight, if they were to die fighting without accomplishing what they wanted their entire lives, dying in the delusion that they ‘sacrificed benevolently’; don’t you think those who behave like this are pretty funny to watch?” The Liberty Queen asked back with a smile.

“That doesn’t mean that the person with empathy is the strongest person!” Yue Yang’s refutation was unparalleled, but this was the first time that he felt a little difficult to refute.

“A person who truly has empathy does not mean that they have to behave like me. I just want to do well, but I can’t evoke true empathy. The greatest empathy is not as simple as letting some disaster victims get food. Here, I will give this example to illustrate that only the most powerful people can truly appreciate the humble when they look down on the common people and truly understand what ’empathy’ is. Those truly powerful ancient Gods are in the sky looking down at us people in the lower world, watching us live our lives as busy as ants, watching us being born, growing, fighting for profit, fighting to the death for fame and fortune, watching us get injured and ageing— all in the struggle for power and profit before succumbing to sickness and death in the end… Only by seeing into all such existences can one truly see the truth of the world. See the deplorability of low-level beings and understand what to do to be truly empathetic to all forms of life!”

When the Liberty Queen said that, Yue Yang was completely shocked.

He felt that he had an enlightened awakening, a feeling he had never felt before, bursting out from the deepest part of his heart.

Before that, he was constantly thinking about what was God and what was human. What was the limit of power and what was the ultimate goal of his growth. After today’s speech, he suddenly realised that he had found the best answer.

Only the most powerful person who stood atop the peak of strength could see the truth of the world, and could appreciate the smallest of beings.

Only then, would there be a form of empathy to look upon others with an extraordinary heart.

If he didn’t understand this, that would have meant that he wasn’t strong enough. But if he could do it, then why would he limit himself to the scopes of those who couldn’t understand? He finally understood it… [1]

[T/L Note: So I’m trying to understand as well, but the gist of it is probably saying that one can only truly empathise if you are strong enough to see the lives of others. To perceive, understand, and to remain grounded— but also know that you are strong enough to bring about change to better someone’s life and empathise with the weak.

Alas, I’m not Yue Yang so I’m not smart enough to understand the full meaning, but this should be something like that]

There was no change to Yue Yang on the surface, but in his heart, an earth-shattering change was taking place.

chapters.title: Chapter 951
chapters.body:
Chapter 951

Chapter 951: The Purest Water, The Most Perfect Fire

In the Grimoire World.

After Yue Yang and the Liberty Queen finished their conversation, he returned to the Grimoire World and sat down.

At first, Princess Qian Qian and Yue Yu didn’t think it was strange as they took care of themselves. They didn’t know how much time passed but when they finally finished practising, they planned to invite student Yue Yang to have a bath together. However, they didn’t expect to see him sitting aside and being immersed deep in thought. They ultimately decided not to bother him because they knew that his thoughts would roam well over two hours on occasion, especially when he was thinking about how to proceed with his new experiments.

Nevertheless, when Drunken Cat Yu Jie finished showering and was planning to rest, she found out from the young slave’s murmurs that Yue Yang had been remaining deep in thought for more than five hours.

This anomaly was something that never occurred before.

What the hell was going on outside?

How could this happen?

When Princess Qian Qian and Yue Yu both found out, they became dumbfounded.

The three girls hid outside the door with their eyes locked on the thin crevice of the door. They whispered while they watched him, “What should we do now? Should we go in and wake him up?”

“If he is in the middle of obtaining some sort of inspiration, then waking him up might put his efforts in vain,” Yue Yu shook her head slightly.

“But the problem is that he has been in a daze for too long,” Princess Qian Qian had never seen Yue Yang being trapped in such a daze.

“Perhaps he is already in the last stage of thoughts. We should believe him,” Among the three women, Yue Yu was the one who believed in Yue Yang the most. She felt that he was not just blankly stuck in a pointless mindset, but was exploring different possible solutions.

“Then, let’s just wait a little longer!” Drunken cat Yu Jie felt like she should actually be supporting him to the best of her abilities.

“Well then, you guys can wait for him. I think I overexerted myself from today’s practice— I’m absolutely worn out at this point,” Princess Qian Qian, too, started to think things over and felt that this matter wasn’t as serious as they all thought it was. He’s just sitting there and thinking about the varied possibilities to approach a problem. But if Xue Wu Xia was here, she might have been able to offer him some sort of advice. Princess Qian Qian knew that her level and his were currently too far, so if she tried to merge her realm with his inner realm, all she was going to get was a splitting headache and an extra bout of nausea.

Hence, she decided not to wait any longer and turned in to rest. At any rate, with Yue Yu watching over him, nothing would happen to him.

After waiting for an hour, Drunken Cat Yu Jie was also starting to fall asleep.

Yue Yu saw that she had been yawning and persuaded her to rest.

Both her and Princess Qian Qian were too diligent in their practice during the day. In order to improve, they overexerted their strengths many times.

“It’s okay, little slave is here to accompany me!” Yue Yu persuaded Drunken Cat Yu Jie to go to sleep as the little slave also said that she would accompany Yue Yu. She sat on the side to accompany Yue Yu to speak in a low voice to relieve her boredom. However, after a while, she comfortably slept in Yue Yu’s embrace, which elicited some laughter from the girl as she stared at her friend’s sleeping figure.

“You guys seriously can’t even let me have a day of peace without worrying for you all,” Yue Yu carried the little slave to the bed and covered the girl gently with a quilt.

If Si-niang wasn’t around, it was her turn to take care of everyone.

She had to especially take care of little brother Xiao San, who was even more afraid of being alone.

If there was no one around to accompany him, he would feel lethargic. Though his powers were getting stronger with every passing moment, he was actually still a little kid in many ways.

Yue Yu would occasionally feel that way, but she would never admit it on the surface. Actually, she was very willing to see her brother in a vulnerable state. It was at times when he especially needed to rely on her that she would feel like they were extremely close because that meant that he was okay with completely letting his guard down around her and trusting her to take care of him. Being with him throughout his journey through life and taking every step with him towards the route of success— it was an indescribably beautiful feeling that she would never trade anything for.

In the dead of night, everyone fell asleep.

Yue Yu sighed slightly when she saw that Yue Yang was still sitting quietly.

Xue Wu Xia was sleeping, Luo Hua and Yi nan were not there; Qian Qian was tired and currently still resting, so there was really no one to help her out.

It actually was getting pretty worrying for him to keep remaining in hisd dazed state. What exactly went on out there? Couldn’t he have told the others and discussed it together instead of keeping it to himself?

“No, this shouldn’t go on any longer!” Yue Yu first looked outside the door and found that everyone was sleeping as she bit her lower lip. She made several decisions in her heart before she finally shook her head and gave up. If she were to merge her thoughts with him and not let him know about it as she disrupted his thoughts, she might even risk her secrets being exposed to him, which wouldn’t be a good thing at all.

However, this was too worrying for her to let this continue. After sitting still for a while longer, Yue Yu felt that she really couldn’t wait any longer.

What couldn’t she help with? She loved Xiao San the most. If he encountered any difficulties, as long as she was capable of helping him solve it, it didn’t matter to her even if she was smashed to pieces. If she couldn’t even help him with this, who was she to say that she would walk this path with him?

With this thought clouding her heart, Yue Yu held her small fist and waved it gently as if she was helping herself to make a decision.

She looked over towards the door— indeed, no one was going to come forward to interrupt him.

She relaxed gradually, knowing that without anyone here, she could help him return to reality.

Once Yue Yu made a decision in her heart, she no longer suppressed her tenderness. She walked over gently, first sitting quietly beside him as she waited for the spiritual energy ribbon to touch his arm with a slight tremble, blending into his. Her mind and hands had already embraced him at some point. Xiao San, who she was willing to sacrifice her life for in exchange, what is it that made you so embarrassed? Couldn’t you tell me? Quietly, in her heart, she wanted him to tell her what was troubling him so much. She wanted to share the burden and not let him carry it alone.

Yue Yu, whose mind was completely immersed in Yue Yang’s thoughts, was surprised to find out that his spiritual world was simply exquisite.

It was as quiet as the sea after a storm. There were no clouds for thousands of miles, whereas the sea and the skies were of the same colour. His entire mind and spiritual being were as vast as the ocean but it remained calm without waves crashing onto its shores.

Yue Yu found that her spiritual body was as pure as a newborn as the figure of her naked frame was very natural, not presenting any sort of physical embarrassment at all. And he, who sat quietly far away, sat in a daze like how he appeared from his outer body.

What happened?

With surprise and some doubts, Yue Yu’s spiritual body drifted towards Yue Yang’s spiritual body, which was also as pure as a newborn child. With waves of concern and comfort, she stretched out her fingers and gently touched his back before she slowly leaned over. Gently, she laid down and leaned on him. Because of the contact, the hearts of the two slowly merged together, at the same time sharing and inquiring about the other party’s secrets. Everything that was meant to be a secret was instantaneously exposed to the other without reserve and mystery.

In an instant, she understood the profound truth of his thinking and the beauty of the ever-improving spiritual realm.

At the same time, he also sensed her concern and worry.

There was also the emotion that was well-hidden in the depths of the heart and he trembled slightly when he thought about it. He knew her, she knew him and she could even feel the comfort, knowing that both their worries were now open to the other.

She could sense the deepest desires and needs of his heart. At this moment, the two of them were like one person and there were no secrets between their spiritual beings at all.

The only regret she had was that her realm was not as high as his.

In many ways, she could sense far less than what he knew.

Just like what Princess Qian Qian said, the dazzling and endless sea of knowledge was not something she could sense at this time— and not all of his secrets were something that she could inquire about. Even if he wanted to share it, she couldn’t do it. The most important thing was that she was unwilling to get a deep understanding of some of his secrets, such as his various emotional exchanges with Xue Wu Xia, Qian Qian, Luo Hua, Yi Nan and Sky Law, which encompassed his more intimate relationships with them. To avoid the awkwardness, she left these private moments for him and the original owner to share alone, instead of curiously scrutinising over them pointlessly. Even so, there were still some spiritual secrets that couldn’t wait to come over and unfortunately for her, she had no time to avoid those memories, such as a certain lover’s double cultivation time. This situation, or the reaction of the couple’s double cultivation, was something that she couldn’t avoid contact with even if she wanted to, so it was inevitable that some sort of embarrassment would arise.

Various changes in the body and the situation during the double cultivation— all these emotions and feelings rushed past her.

His heart was merged with hers.

Yue Yu felt that she had flown off somewhere. It felt as if she had broken through the ceiling, ventured beyond the skies where a big hole had been drilled through. Looking back, she glanced down at the world that was completely invisible to her. This feeling was something that she had never experienced before in her entire lifetime.

Was it fear? Surprise?

There seemed to be a curious and detached form of joy as well!

Why did Xiao San bring himself to experience this wonderful feeling? Was this his latest insight? Or was it his worry?

“If I can help you, I would…but, I don’t know what to do!” Yue Yu’s spiritual body hoped that she could help him. Whether it was a problem or figuring out a profound meaning, she was willing to cooperate however he wished as long as she could help him in some way.

“…” Yue Yang’s spiritual body did not answer her; instead, it just flashed with a burst of energy.

Needless to say, she knew he would agree.

And she didn’t know what to do, but she knew that he would use her well, so she placed her trust completely in him.

The hearts of the two people were completely integrated again. This time, instead of communicating like they did before, they were engaged in a new kind of cultivation. This was Yue Yang’s cultivation after her enlightenment that was not only for the mind, but also for the body. The White Splendid Frost of the Innate Sword Qi shone, the clothes of the two were turned into flying specks of ash. Their bodies hugged each other tightly and the energy of pure yin to yang attributes merged with each other.

One side was the purest water, whereas on the other was the most perfect fire.

Such an exchange occurred and there was no discordant destruction nor incorrect conflict. There was only a more beautiful union and a more beautiful sublimation.

Both minds and bodies were in this sublimation as they were constantly improving.

The huge energy column shot straight into the sky as if it was piercing through the skies of the entire Grimoire World. Drunken Cat Yu Jie and Princess Qian Qian woke up from their dreams and stared dumbfoundedly at the energy beams under Yue Yu’s energy supplement. It was only now that they realised how high Yue Yang’s enlightenment breakthrough was this time and how big his improvement was.

The little slave quietly made a ‘shush’ gesture, indicating that this practice should continue as they shouldn’t wake them up.

Princess Qian Qian smiled slightly as she looked at Drunken Cat Yu Jie. She stretched out her cute waist and continued to go back to rest, assuming that none of this was happening.

The next day, Yue Yu, whose body and mind were greatly cleansed and sublimated, woke up in the spiritual world that she blended and merged in.

She found herself atop Xiao San’s body.

A bad hand touched her forbidden area very restlessly, which startled her.

She got up quickly, feeling a little embarrassed and a little worried but secretly relieved. Fortunately, his movement just came across as a shock and wasn’t really trying to do anything.

Yue Yu, who had put on her clothes, quickly took his clothes and put them on him while he was still sleeping in his sweet dreams. However, an arrogant and aroused ‘bad thing’ had to erect itself and make her feel embarrassed. Fortunately, no one saw it, otherwise, she would have felt really ashamed. Holding back her burning face, she covered him with a quilt and then looked outside the door. Upon finding that no one got up and everyone was still asleep, she breathed out a sigh of relief and was glad that she woke early.

With the joy of helping him improve his successful breakthrough, she quietly opened the door and slipped out.

Trying to resist the urge to hum a tune while suppressing her joy, she started to cook and make him breakfast.

An hour later, when she had everything ready and came in early with a warm and loving face, she happened to see him get up sleepily and say, “It’s so fragrant.”

She replied to him, “Slackers who don’t wash their faces and don’t rinse their mouths will only watch others eat their breakfast…”

If she could do this everyday, she would actually use her entire life to behave as such. That was because this worrying brat really couldn’t afford to be uncared for even a day!

chapters.title: Chapter 952
chapters.body:
Chapter 952

Chapter 952: Why Don’t You Go And Steal It?

On the luxurious airship heading for the Wind-stopping Swamp, when Yue Yang came out of the room, his eyes scanned around the space and found the Liberty Queen in the back hall.

The Heaven Rankers who went together with them were either cultivating like Sky Execution or were gathered in the front hall to hold a reception. The sound of laughter and applause broke out, making Yue Yang quite dizzy. How did this appear as if they were going to lift a curse? They seemed like they were going to attend a wedding ceremony instead!

“You look completely different,” The Liberty Queen had been meditating for a while. After Yue Yang came out, she suddenly turned around to ‘look’ at Yue Yang and exclaimed.

“What’s the difference?” Yue Yang thought it was quite amusing— just what could she see with her eyes?

“Of course, this is not something that one can see on the surface, and even the feeling in your heart is very vague. But I think that you were born with a very natural ability to conceal yourself. However, your soul has a brilliance that can’t be concealed as it is simply brilliant. I have contacted brilliant souls like yours before as there have been some powerhouses who were very close to the level of the Gods. But when I compare their brilliance with yours, the drastic difference between the quality is like comparing the luminance of a firefly with that of the full moon. It seems that you have found the right path through the realm of the Gods,” Liberty Queen said so and Yue Yang was secretly surprised. In his entire journey, he had only met a few people who possessed the ability to see through his own talents to conceal his abilities.

“Well, I would like to thank you for your words of inspiration from your high level of enlightenment,” Yue Yang greeted the Liberty Queen slightly, but rather sincerely.

“It’s not my enlightenment, it’s a kind of epiphany that finally spewed out in the process of your growth— an explosion after infinite accumulation, if you will. And in your path of continuous exploration, this was a necessity, but it’s simply not because of my appearance nor my words,” The Liberty Queen had a smile on her face.

“In any case, it’s because of your words that offered me enlightenment,” Yue Yang saw that the Liberty Queen didn’t want to take credit, so he didn’t mention it. He asked, “Can I talk about you?”

“Me?” The Liberty Queen seemed a little surprised by this topic.

“Have you never thought about yourself?” Yue Yang wondered.

“I have, and I often think about what I will do in the future or what is the meaning of doing something. Like you, I am also exploring the meaning of life and trying to experience a higher realm. For example, just now, I was still thinking about a question,” The Liberty Queen nodded, talking about herself with the ‘stranger’ Yue Yang for the first time.

“What were you thinking about just now?” Yue Yang asked again.

“I was thinking about the best thing to do. Would it be to stick to my original beliefs, or to be like you, quietly watching the crowd around me in a higher realm and watch them rush like ants without lifting a finger to interfere,” The Liberty Queen replied.

“Ha, isn’t this kind of behaviour that doesn’t lift a finger rather indifferent?” Yue Yang smiled.

“For a life with only selfish and vulgar thoughts at a low realm, it is indeed a kind of ruthlessness— a kind of selfishness and egoistic indifference. But standing at a higher and farther realm, it will be a little bit difficult to see the problem. It’s not the same, but that’s not something selfish people can understand. It’s like an ordinary person who interferes with the life of a group of ants. If one gives them food and protects them, ants will still be ants and they have not achieved real essential changes. If the ants were suffering, giving them food and protection would only prolong their pain and wouldn’t really allow them to obtain real happiness and relief… Life is very insignificant to the whole world and all of life’s existence is the embodiment of a will of the world. Using one life’s ability to influence another life may be a manifestation of the world’s will. But perhaps, this might just be the wrong execution as you can’t be exempted from the world’s will and you can never understand the truth of the world. Actually, the more you do, the more likely things might go wrong. That’s a question that I have been thinking about, and I’ve found that the more capable beings are more likely to ignore the low-level beings. Why didn’t the ancient Gods relieve the suffering of all humble beings? Why didn’t the ancient Gods give the weak life a type of powerful power? If the ancient Gods wanted to do it, then they could do it as long as they set a rule for it. But they didn’t …” The Liberty Queen’s words once again shocked Yue Yang, who once traversed time and space to get here.

“If that’s the case, why do you have to pay for others unconditionally?” Yue Yang wanted to figure out the answer to this question the most.

“Because I’m not an ancient God. I don’t stand at a higher and farther realm to look at problems. When I sense the pain of people around me, I can’t help but be emotional. I don’t have the will to move and I’m in a very low realm, but my heart is easily infected by others. When I see how unfortunate the people around me are, and how eager they are in need of help, I can’t help but offer my aid… Sometimes I know that what I’m doing is wrong, but sometimes, I don’t care. Because no matter what I do, I am still driven by my heart to do something even if it is wrong,” The Liberty Queen explained.

“Do you want to do it even if you know it’s wrong? Maybe you don’t have the unshakable will of the Innate Supreme being, but you have a kindness that others don’t have, a kind of compassion that is still willing to help the suffering even though you understand the truth. I don’t know how to say it. Maybe, you are like a manifestation of the will of the world. This world needs all kinds of beings— there are those who are indifferent like me and there are those who are kind-hearted like you,” Yue Yang pondered for a long time before answering affirmatively.

“Really? Hearing you comfort me, I feel an unprecedented warmth in my heart,” The Liberty Queen smiled and her face burst into a beautiful brilliance that stemmed from the bottom of her heart.

“Have you ever thought about death?” Yue Yang hesitated slightly before he finally asked.

“In fact, I have thought about this question. Although as long as my Divine Power is not exhausted, I will not die. However, you know that if I go on like this, one day, my life will come to an end. I am not afraid of death. In fact, my current life is more painful than death; perhaps death would be a rare form of relief for me!” The Liberty Queen asked him, “Have you ever thought about death?”

“No, I am afraid of death so I try not to think about it. As long as I can live for one day longer, I will insist on living, haha!” Yue Yang laughed.

“As soon as you start laughing, what you say won’t be the truth,” The Liberty Queen seemed to be able to see through Yue Yang’s facade rather easily.

“Cough, I’m just a normal cultivator; it’s normal for me to be afraid of death…” Yue Yang was a little embarrassed and it made him appear really sweaty. If it was another person, Yue Yang might have split the other party with the Crescent Moon blade. But for some reason, he really couldn’t attack the Liberty Queen, who had been nothing but honest and open with him all along.

“Maybe at the beginning, you would be afraid of death, which would be something that all forms of life feared, but today, with your unshakable will and indestructible power, you probably haven’t thought about this issue for a long time. I can’t see through the truth about you, but I can vaguely feel that you are an existence beyond the will of the entire world. In the future, you may create a world of your own… So how can an existence like you be afraid of death? You only empathised with my sorry state of a life because my appearance moved your enclosed heart; is that it? Even if you deny it, I will still be very happy. After all, there is someone who cares about me!” The suspended body of the Liberty Queen gave a slight salute to Yue Yang.

“Does it have to go on like this?” Yue Yang decided to ask his last question.

“I don’t know, but what can someone like me do other than this? You have a clear goal, but I, by the way, don’t mind if you laugh at me because of it, I don’t have my own goal in life. So if I don’t do something, I feel very confused,” For the first time, the Liberty Queen showed a charming gesture like a little girl. At that moment, she didn’t seem to be the Liberty Queen, but a confused and curious little girl.

“I understand, I’ll see you later,” Yue Yang also had a smile on his face. This natural expression of hers was far too cute. He resisted the urge to pinch her cheek slightly before he waved his hand and strode towards the hall.

“What a strange person… an awkward personality, but a trustworthy person.”

The Liberty Queen stroked her left cheek.

Her actions appeared to be as if Yue Yang had actually pinched her cheek as she kept her hand on her left cheek.

Yue Yang went to the front hall and looked around. He found that Ming Ri Hao was not there, so he used his senses and found that the guy was sleeping in a room.Without the courtesy of a warning, Yue Yang used his leg to kick open the door.

Ming Ri Hao opened his eyes and looked at student Yue Yang dumbfoundedly, not knowing when he provoked this kid for him to be this dramatic.

Especially with Yue Yang staring at him, he felt even more guiltier despite the fact that he hadn’t done anything, “No matter what happened, I believe it has nothing to do with me— I was just sleeping!”

Yue Yang snorted coldly, “It goes without saying that Zhu Guang is the fugitive slave from your family; even the blind can see your change in appearance. Now, I just want to know, what is her relationship with you? Don’t tell me that an ordinary fugitive slave will have a Prayer Bead that was at a level of a Quasi-Divine Artifact on her body. Don’t use the set of rhetorical words that are meant to coax children on me either. If it was truly an ordinary master-servant relationship, would you have left everything behind and come here all the way to hunt? Moreover, if you had already seen her and not arrested her directly, what are you trying to hide and steal?”

Ming Ri Hao broke out in a sweat, “Can’t I come for the Divine Grimoire? And arrest the fugitive slave on the way?”

Yue Yang gave him two bumps and said with extreme contempt, “Do you think that those equipped with a normal surge of intelligence would believe your explanation? Anyways, my intelligence is more than double of yours, alright?”

Ming Ri Hao was angry.

In terms of intelligence, no matter how highly smart he was, did he have to say that it was doubled?

Damn it, he was pretty smart, that’s true; but would it kill him to not flaunt it and belittle those that were less intelligent?

He was about to retort, but student Yue Yang spoke out, “If you don’t answer the question directly, then this Young Master will lead the team back to the Tong Tian Tower. I just happened to forget that there is an important matter that has not been done yet, so you should slowly clean up this mess.”

How else could Ming Ri Hao deal with him? In the face of such a shameless guy, he had no choice but to admit it, “Forget it, let’s just say, in fact, Zhu Guang is one of the important attendants by my sister’s side. She was the one responsible for going back to ask for reinforcements in the battle, but the reinforcements never came. The Prison Emperor was then seriously injured after a sneak attack by a powerhouse who appeared inexplicably. In the next battle, we were all sealed in the Prison Emperor’s Palace by the Three Divine Artifacts. So you see, do you think I can just leave Zhu Guang out there, who is a runaway slave that may be related to the Central Palace?”

Yue Yang clapped his hands, “Okay, okay, what a bad excuse! Let me guess? I suspect that Zhu Guang back then was your little lover or something. Otherwise, why would you send a maid you don’t trust back to ask for reinforcements? Except for parents and children. In addition to blood relationships between parent and child, or family members, the relationship between adulterers and prostitutes are the second strongest, so you don’t need to argue any more. I’m not blind, let alone a fool. I can ignore the fact that Zhu Guang is your little lover, but I do want the Prayer Bead Quasi-Divine Artifact on her head. Ah, why don’t you pretend that this is one of the dowries that you’ll present to me when Ming Yue Guang marries me?”

“Why don’t you go and steal it?” Ming Ri Hao was so angry that he almost vomited out a puddle of blood. He had never seen such a person in his life. This kid was so shameless— how did he remain unpunished by the heavens?

chapters.title: Chapter 953
chapters.body:
Chapter 953

Chapter 953: Ambush, At A Terrifying Speed

Wind-stopping Swamp.

This was a very special place. Besides the fact that there was no wind present at any point of time, every other aspect was relatively normal.

However, precisely because there was no wind at all times turned this swampy area with large forests, rivers, and lakes into areas that were so grotesque and terrifying. All kinds of alienated monsters that were completely invisible outside the Wind-stopping Swamp live roam around everywhere. As long as there were outsiders present, those creatures wouldn’t hesitate to attack and hunt them.

Other than those who were Heaven Ranked powerhouses and above, anyone lower than those levels would only find the Wind-stopping Swamp to become the place of their deathbeds.

Luxury airships could only stop at the periphery of the territory.

Without the wind, even if they could continue to fly forward, the ship would be slower than a turtle crawling.

The power of the law that forbids any form of wind brought about an indescribable control over the entire area of the swamp. Any object entering will be affected unexpectedly.

“There is no problem with the air here and we can breathe quite easily,” Yue Yang found that this Wind-stopping Swamp was really weird. You could breathe, but it couldn’t produce any ‘wind’? Exhaling here was as strenuous as exhaling in water, perhaps even more difficult. On the contrary, the inhalation process was surprisingly smooth, giving them an illusion that the inhalation of air into the lungs is not unpleasant.

“Look!” Ming Ri Hao poured a glass of water into his hand.

The water in the cup that poured down, like a trickle of a soft waterfall, flowed down.

A lot of water droplets splashed away, gliding slowly under the sun, gradually forming a beautiful rainbow.

On the surface, everything looked wonderful, but Yue Yang, Sky Execution and the others could clearly tell that this place was very abnormal. The speed of the flow of water and the water droplets falling down here was way too slow! Yue Yang felt like he was watching the water droplets from a movie with how slow it was moving. It was completely visible to the eye and he could see every detail clearly.

Sky Execution, the Dragon Emperor and the Underworld Emperor looked at each other and stretched out their hands towards the sky.

A silver lightning flashed out between his fingers.

Like an electric eel, the lighting streaks swum beautifully in the sky, and each arc and every spark evolving elegantly for a long time without disappearing.

Yue Yang looked at it and his face became solemn— it seemed like the impact of having no ‘wind’ present was rather prominent for battle. For someone who wielded lightning like Sky Execution, his usual power was at least 500 or maybe even 1000 times faster than what he showed just now. Otherwise, how would it have been possible for him to strike his enemies? Although water and electricity were not prohibited by the law’s power itself, the effect of not having wind had definitely more or less affected the speed and the quality of their attacks.

The Underworld Emperor continuously waved his fists and pretended to attack an imaginary enemy in front of him.

Because there was no wind, the speed of his punches slowed down by at least 10%.

At the same time, the strengths of his powers have also been weakened a lot.

The Dragon Emperor pondered for a while before he finally condensed a ball of light and threw it into the sky.

The shock wave of the light ball disappeared almost as if it was never generated. However, the speed of the scattered light was far beyond everyone’s expectations. It spreaded out faster than the original instance and disappeared instantly, not knowing where the scattering luminance went off to.

“I didn’t expect you to notice it so quickly. Except for light and darkness, other elements are negatively affected here and their power is greatly reduced. Although light and dark are not affected, they have evolved beyond the original control range and become an uncontrollable existence. In short, if a warrior who purely used their elemental power is here, it will be a catastrophic encounter,” Ming Ri Hao frowned slightly as he knew that the person he was referring to, was himself.

Although he hid his strength and to the outer world, he wasn’t an elemental user, he was at his best when he wielded fire and light. But here, the fire was greatly weakened and the light elements were uncontrollable, which greatly affected his strength.

Sky Execution clenched his fists.

The effect that he suffered was not small. As for the Underworld Emperor, at this point, he just wanted to find a corner and draw circles on the floor. Almost all of his abilities depended on dark elements. Now that it was bound to go out of control he could only be nothing more than a passer-by. Thankfully, the Dragon Emperor was in a much better state. Although he also used his fair share of elemental power, the Dragon Emperor was still an oriental monster, so his strong body was also one of the main components of his combat power.

Of course, among many people, only the fellow Yue Yang could laugh heartlessly.

No matter how influential the effect was, there was absolutely no problem when it came to self-protection for those who knew how to use the power of the small law and possessed the field of creations.

Ming Ri Hao hated the brat so much that his teeth were clenched and he wanted to beat this kid up, but he couldn’t do anything about it. At this point, he was adamant on the thought of hanging himself.

When the Heaven Rankers opened the way, Yue Yang flew slowly in the middle of the team, movements as leisurely as a mere outing.

Except for him, no one else could be that relaxed.
